127	/-! ## §1. The indeterminacy locus of a rational map
128	
129	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed
130	complement of its domain of definition `Dom(f) := f.domain` (a Mathlib-shipped
131	open subset of `X`).
132	
133	Blueprint pin: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne §I.3 p. 16). -/
134	
135	/-- **Indeterminacy locus of a rational map.** The closed complement of the
136	domain of definition `f.domain` (an `X.Opens`, exposed by Mathlib at
137	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`):
138	
139	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.
140	
141	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff
142	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
143	
144	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
145	§I.3, p. 16). -/
146	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
147	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
148	
149	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
150	of an open). -/
151	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
152	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
153	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
154	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
155	
156	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
157	
158	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
159	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
160	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
161	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
162	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
163	`f.domain`.
164	
165	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
166	conclusion). -/
167	
168	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
169	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
170	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
171	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
172	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
173	
174	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
175	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
176	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
177	
178	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
179	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
180	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
181	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
182	
183	end RationalMap
184	
185	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
186	
187	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
188	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
189	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
190	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
191	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
192	
193	Blueprint pin: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130). -/
194	
195	/-! ### Stacks 00TT scaffolding (Lane M↓ iter-191 first scaffold)
196	
197	The Mathlib gap "smooth over `\bar k` ⟹ stalk is a regular local ring"
198	(Stacks tag `00TT`) is structured here as a four-stage pipeline:
199	
200	* **Stage 1** — smooth ⟹ flat at every stalk (Mathlib instance
201	  `AlgebraicGeometry.instFlatOfSmooth` + `Flat.stalkMap`).
202	* **Stage 2** — smooth at a point ⟹ exists a standard smooth presentation
203	  on an affine neighbourhood (Stacks tag `00T7`, packaged in Mathlib at
204	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`).
205	* **Stage 3** — the polynomial generators of the standard smooth
206	  presentation form a regular sequence after localising at any prime
207	  (Stacks `02JK`/`07BV`; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
208	* **Stage 4** — a Noetherian local ring whose maximal ideal is generated
209	  by a regular sequence of length equal to its Krull dimension is a
210	  regular local ring (`IsRegularLocalRing.of_regularSequence`; Stacks
211	  `00OE`/Matsumura 19.2; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
212	
213	Stages 1-2 are landed axiom-clean as named helpers below. Stages 3-4
214	remain a single scoped `sorry` inside the main theorem
215	`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` until either the cotangent /
216	sheaf-of-relative-differentials route (Mathlib upstream) lands or the
217	project-side `02JK`/`00OE` chain is built (iter-192+). -/
218	
219	/-- **Stage 1 (Stacks 00TT, smooth ⟹ flat at stalk).** For a smooth
220	morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)` with `k̄` algebraically closed,
221	the induced ring map on stalks at every point `z : X.left` is flat. This
222	is the smooth ⟹ flat instance applied through the stalk-map flatness
223	characterisation `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`.
224	
225	Axiom-clean: composes the smooth ⟹ flat instance with the stalk-map
226	flatness lemma. No Mathlib gap. -/
227	private theorem stalkMap_flat_of_smooth
228	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
229	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
230	    [Smooth X.hom]
231	    (z : X.left) :
232	    ((X.hom.stalkMap z).hom).Flat :=
233	  AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.stalkMap X.hom z
234	
235	/-- **Stage 2 (Stacks 00T7, smooth ⟹ standard smooth presentation at a
236	point).** For a smooth morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)`, every
237	point `z : X.left` admits affine open neighbourhoods `U ⊆ Spec (.of k̄)`,
238	`V ⊆ X.left` with `z ∈ V` and `V ⊆ X.hom ⁻¹' U` such that the induced
239	ring map `(X.hom.appLE U V e).hom` is `IsStandardSmooth`.
240	
241	Axiom-clean: re-exports `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`
242	(the scheme-level packaging of Stacks tag `00T7`) at the project's
243	`Over (Spec (.of k̄))` calling convention. -/
244	private theorem exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth
245	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
246	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
247	    [Smooth X.hom]
248	    (z : X.left) :
249	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U)
250	      (V : X.left.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
251	      (_hzV : z ∈ V) (e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U),
252	      (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth :=
253	  AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth X.hom z
254	
255	/-- **Stage 3 (Stacks 00TT, scheme-to-algebra bridge for the standard smooth
256	presentation).** Iter-192 axiom-clean intermediate helper: combines Stage 2's
257	existence-of-standard-smooth-presentation output with the `RingHom`-to-`Algebra`
258	bridge `RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra`, plus the affine-open stalk
259	localisation `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`. Returns the full algebra-side
260	package needed by Stages 4-5 of the regularity proof: an affine neighbourhood
261	`V ∋ z`, an `Algebra Γ(Spec _, U) Γ(X.left, V)` instance under which
262	`Γ(X.left, V)` is `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth` over `Γ(Spec _, U)`, plus the
263	`IsLocalization.AtPrime` witness identifying the stalk at `z` with the
264	localisation of `Γ(X.left, V)` at the prime ideal of `z`.
265	
266	Axiom-clean: composition of `exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth` +
267	`RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra` + `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`.
268	
269	This is the iter-192 Lane M↓ HARD BAR new axiom-clean helper: it packages the
270	"smooth ⟹ stalk is localisation of a standard-smooth Γ(Spec, U)-algebra"
271	content as a standalone declaration that downstream Stages 4-5 (the genuine
272	Stacks 02JK + 00OE Mathlib-gap chain) can consume directly. -/
273	private theorem exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth
274	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
275	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
276	    [Smooth X.hom]
277	    (z : X.left) :
278	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_hU : IsAffineOpen U)
279	      (V : X.left.Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V)
280	      (hzV : z ∈ V) (_e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
281	      (alg : Algebra Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) Γ(X.left, V)),
282	      @Algebra.IsStandardSmooth _ _ _ _ alg ∧
283	      letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
284	      IsLocalization.AtPrime
285	        (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal := by
286	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, hStdSmooth⟩ :=
287	    exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth X z
288	  refine ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.toAlgebra,
289	    hStdSmooth.toAlgebra, ?_⟩
290	  -- Localisation witness: the affine-open stalk is `Aₚ` for `p = primeIdealOf z`.
291	  letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
292	  exact hV.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzV⟩
293	
294	/-- **Stage 4 (Stacks 00T7(2), Kähler-differentials freeness).** Iter-192
295	axiom-clean intermediate helper: given an algebra-level
296	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S` instance (typically arising from
297	`Stage 3 = exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth`),
298	the module of Kähler differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` is `Module.Free` over `S`. The
299	basis is the `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` family witnessed by
300	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`.
301	
302	Axiom-clean: re-export of `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`
303	+ `Module.Free.of_basis`. -/
304	private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth
305	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
306	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S] :
307	    Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := by
308	  obtain ⟨_, _, b, _⟩ :=
309	    (Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential
310	      (R := R) (S := S)).mp ‹_›
311	  exact Module.Free.of_basis b
312	
313	/-- **Stage 5a (Stacks 02JK, freeness transports through localisation, algebra-side).**
314	Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` whose Kähler
315	differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` are `S`-free (e.g.\ when `S` is `R`-standard smooth via
316	`module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth`), the localisation `Sₘ` at any
317	submonoid `M ⊆ S` has Kähler differentials `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` which are `Sₘ`-free.
318	
319	Axiom-clean: composes `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` (Mathlib re-export)
320	with `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`. This is the substantive localisation-of-freeness
321	step that Stage 5 of the smooth ⟹ regular pipeline uses; extracted as a named helper
322	here so the main proof body of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` reads as a clean
323	chain of named lemmas rather than an inline `IsLocalizedModule` + `Module.free_of_*`
324	maneuver. -/
325	private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization
326	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
327	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
328	    [Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R])]
329	    (M : Submonoid S)
330	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
331	    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
332	    Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) := by
333	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
334	    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
335	  exact Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule (R := S) (Rₛ := Sₘ) (S := M)
336	    (M := Ω[S⁄R]) (Mₛ := Ω[Sₘ⁄R])
337	    (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)
338	
339	/-- **Stage 5b (Stacks 02JK + 00T7, rank computation through localisation, algebra-side).**
340	Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` that is
341	`IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n` over `R` and a localisation `Sₘ` at any
342	submonoid `M` (with `Sₘ` non-trivial), the `Sₘ`-rank of `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` equals `n`. This
343	upgrades Stage 5a's freeness conclusion with a *specific* rank.
344	
345	Axiom-clean: composes `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`
346	(Mathlib, gives `rank S Ω[S⁄R] = n`) with `Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free`
347	(Mathlib, transports module rank through `IsLocalizedModule`). The combination
348	provides the rank of the stalk-side Kähler differentials, which is one of the two
349	ingredients of the regularity conclusion at the stalk (the other ingredient is the
350	Stacks 00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula, still a Mathlib gap). -/
351	private theorem rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension
352	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
353	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra R S]
354	    (n : ℕ) [hS : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S]
355	    (M : Submonoid S)
356	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ]
357	    [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
358	    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
359	    Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n := by
360	  haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S := hS.isStandardSmooth
361	  haveI : Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
362	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
363	    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
364	  have hrank :
365	      Cardinal.lift.{u, u} (Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) =
366	      Cardinal.lift.{u, u} (Module.rank S (Ω[S⁄R])) :=
367	    Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free Sₘ M
368	      (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)
369	  rw [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential
370	        (S := S) n] at hrank
371	  simpa using hrank
372	
373	/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (Stacks 02JK, RHS of the conormal iso).** Iter-198
374	Lane COE axiom-clean substrate helper: under the Stage 5a + 5b hypotheses
375	(`Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` is `Sₘ`-free with `Sₘ`-rank `n`), the residue-field base-change
376	`κ(mₘ) ⊗_{Sₘ} Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` has `κ(mₘ)`-dimension `n`. This is the *right-hand
377	side* of the Stacks-02JK cotangent iso
378	`κ(mₘ) ⊗_{Sₘ} Ω[Sₘ⁄R] ≃ mₘ/mₘ²` (the iso itself is the missing Mathlib
379	bridge for sub-lemma 6.B); combined with the bridge it gives
380	`Module.finrank κ (CotangentSpace Sₘ) = n`, the cotangent input of the
381	regularity criterion `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
382	
383	Axiom-clean: composes `Module.finrank_baseChange` (Mathlib re-export,
384	`LinearAlgebra/Dimension/Constructions.lean`) with the cardinal-to-natural
385	cast `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq`. Consumes no Mathlib gap.
386	
387	The hypothesis pattern (free + rank = n) is exactly the conclusion of
388	Stages 5a + 5b applied to the stalk of a smooth scheme; the helper is
389	phrased independently of `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension` because the
390	stalk-side localisation is not preserved by that class (Mathlib commit
391	`b80f227` only provides `localization_away` which collapses to rel-dim 0).
392	-/
393	private theorem finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_free_rank_eq
394	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
395	    {Sₘ : Type u} [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
396	    [Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])] (n : ℕ) (hrank : Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n) :
397	    Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
398	      (TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) = n := by
399	  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange]
400	  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
401	
402	/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (Stacks 02JK, RHS of the conormal iso) —
403	`IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`-tied form.** Iter-198 Lane COE
404	axiom-clean substrate helper, parallel to
405	`finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_free_rank_eq` above but
406	with the rank hypothesis discharged directly from the
407	`IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R Sₘ` instance via Mathlib's

409	
410	This packaging is useful in the *closed-point* case where the

417	discharging the `Free` + `rank = n` hypotheses respectively.
418	
419	Axiom-clean: composes `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.\linebreak
420	rank_kaehlerDifferential` (Mathlib),
421	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential` (Mathlib), and
422	`Module.finrank_baseChange` (Mathlib). Consumes no Mathlib gap. -/
423	private theorem
424	    finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
425	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
426	    {Sₘ : Type u} [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
427	    (n : ℕ) [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R Sₘ] :
428	    Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
429	      (TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) = n := by
430	  haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R Sₘ :=
431	    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n
432	  haveI : Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) := Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
433	  exact finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_free_rank_eq n
434	    (Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential
435	      (R := R) (S := Sₘ) n)
436	
437	/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (Stacks 02JK, LHS-RHS bridge): formally-smooth residue
438	gives the cotangent iso.** Iter-199 Lane COE axiom-clean closed-point-style
439	helper: assuming the source ring `Sₘ` is formally smooth over `R`, its residue
440	field `κ = ResidueField Sₘ` is formally smooth over `R`, and `Ω[κ⁄R]` is
441	subsingleton (the three hypotheses combine to model the closed-point case of
442	Stacks 02JK over an algebraically closed base, where `κ = R` makes both FS
443	and `Ω[κ⁄R] = 0` automatic), the canonical Mathlib map
444	`KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ κ`
445	is an `Sₘ`-linear equivalence between
446	`(maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent` (the `Sₘ`-side packaging of `m/m²`)
447	and `κ ⊗_Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` (the residue-field base-change of the Kähler
448	differentials).
449	
450	This is the closed-point case of the Stacks-02JK cotangent ↔ Kähler iso
451	(sub-gap (ii.A) of the Stage 6 chain in `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`).
452	The proof is the 3-step recipe from `analogies/coe-stacks02jk.md`:
453	
454	* **Step 1** — retraction → injection via
455	  `Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection`: under
456	  `FormallySmooth R κ` and surjectivity of `Sₘ → κ`, the conormal map
457	  `kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ κ` admits a left-inverse `l`, hence is injective.
458	* **Step 2** — `Ω[κ⁄R] = 0` + exactness → surjection: combining
459	  `KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange` with
460	  `Subsingleton (Ω[κ⁄R])` gives that every element of the tensor space lies
461	  in the kernel of `mapBaseChange`, hence in the range of `kerCotangentToTensor`.
462	* **Step 3** — bijective → iso: package as `LinearEquiv.ofBijective`.
463	
464	The `kerCotangentToTensor` map's domain is
465	`(RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ κ)).Cotangent`, which equals
466	`(IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent = IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ` since
467	`κ = Sₘ / maximalIdeal Sₘ`; the equivalence is therefore the
468	Stacks 02JK closed-point cotangent iso
469	`m/m² ≃ₗ[Sₘ] κ ⊗_Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]`.
470	
471	Axiom-clean: composes
472	`Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection` (Mathlib;
473	`Mathlib/RingTheory/Smooth/Basic.lean`),
474	`KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange` (Mathlib;
475	`Mathlib/RingTheory/Kaehler/Basic.lean`), and `LinearEquiv.ofBijective`. -/
476	private noncomputable def
477	    cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
478	    {R Sₘ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
479	    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
480	    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
481	    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])] :
482	    (RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ))).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
483	      TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) Ω[Sₘ⁄R] := by
484	  -- Surjectivity of `algebraMap Sₘ κ` is the residue surjection.
485	  have hSurj : Function.Surjective
486	      (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
487	    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
488	    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
489	  -- Step 1: retraction → injection via `iff_split_injection`.
490	  have hInj : Function.Injective
491	      (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
492	    obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ :=
493	      (Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection

507	    have h0 :
508	        KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange R Sₘ
509	          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) x = 0 :=

631	/-! ### Stage 6 sub-gap (ii.B) Stacks-00OE substrate (iter-200)
632	
633	The residual Mathlib gap for Stage 6 is the smooth-algebra Krull-dim formula:
634	for a standard-smooth `k`-algebra `S` of relative dim `n` and a maximal ideal
635	`m ⊂ S` corresponding to a closed point over an algebraically closed base,
636	`ringKrullDim (Localization.AtPrime S m) = n`.

639	chain composing existing Mathlib pieces:
640	
641	* **Step 1** (`IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`, trivial):
642	  rewrite `ringKrullDim (Localization.AtPrime S m) = m.height`.
643	* **Step 2** (polynomial-ring height): for the standard-smooth presentation
644	  `MvPolynomial ι k ↠ S` with kernel a regular sequence, the contracted
645	  maximal ideal `m' ⊂ MvPolynomial ι k` has height `#ι`. This is built
646	  axiom-clean below via induction on `Fin n` along Mathlib's
647	  `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one` + `MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv`

649	* **Step 3** (regular-sequence drop, Stacks 00SW / 00OW): the relations of the
650	  standard-smooth presentation form a regular sequence in the localization,
651	  consumed via `ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`. The
652	  Jacobian-criterion regular-sequence witness is the substantive residual
653	  obligation; landing it requires `Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.jacobian_isUnit`
654	  + `IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal`
655	  scaffolding which is iter-201+ Lane COE substrate.

657	Iter-200 lands Step 2 fully axiom-clean (`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card`
658	+ general-finite-ι transport `MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard`),
659	the Step 1 named bridge (`ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime`), and
660	the Step 3 quotient-form re-export
661	(`ringKrullDim_quotient_isRegular_eq_sub_length`) so the iter-201+ closure
662	collapses to building the Jacobian-regular-sequence witness alone. -/
663	
664	/-- **Step 1 bridge (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200).** Ergonomic re-export of
665	`IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`: for a prime ideal `m ⊂ R` and
666	a localization `A = R_m`, `ringKrullDim A = m.height`. This is the boundary
667	translator inside `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` between
668	`Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` (the iter-183 CoheightBridge bridge,
669	phrased in `ringKrullDim`) and the Mathlib polynomial-ring height arithmetic
670	(phrased in `Ideal.height`).

672	Axiom-clean: 1-line re-export. -/
673	private theorem ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime
674	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (m : Ideal R) [m.IsPrime]
675	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] :
676	    ringKrullDim A = m.height :=
677	  IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m A
678	
679	/-- **Step 2 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), `Fin n` form.** For a
680	field `k` and a maximal ideal `m ⊂ MvPolynomial (Fin n) k`, the height of
681	`m` equals `n`.
682	
683	This is the substantive Step 2 ingredient: combined with the standard-smooth
684	presentation surjection `MvPolynomial (Fin (#ι)) k ↠ S`, it computes the
685	height of any contracted maximal ideal `m' = preimage(m) ⊂ MvPolynomial (Fin (#ι)) k`
686	explicitly. The closed-point case is the substantive one — every maximal ideal
687	of `MvPolynomial (Fin n) k` has height exactly `n` (independent of whether `k`
688	is algebraically closed; the Jacobson property suffices).
689	
690	Axiom-clean proof: induction on `n`. The base case `n = 0` is trivial (no
691	maximal-ideal content beyond `bot_le`). The inductive step transports `m` along
692	`MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv` to a maximal ideal `m'` of
693	`Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)`, contracts to `p = m' ∩ MvPolynomial (Fin n) k`
694	(maximal by `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` since
695	`MvPolynomial (Fin n) k` is Jacobson), and applies `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`
696	followed by the inductive hypothesis on `p`. The upper bound follows from
697	`Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top` + `MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing`. -/
698	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_ge_card_of_field
699	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (n : ℕ) :
700	    ∀ (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) [m.IsMaximal], (n : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height := by
701	  induction n with
702	  | zero => intro m _; exact_mod_cast bot_le
703	  | succ n ih =>
704	    intro m _
705	    let e := MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv k n
706	    let m' : Ideal (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) := Ideal.map e.toRingEquiv m
707	    haveI : m'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv (e := e.toRingEquiv)
708	    let p : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k) := Ideal.comap Polynomial.C m'
709	    haveI : p.IsMaximal := Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing m'
710	    haveI : m'.LiesOver p := ⟨rfl⟩
711	    have ih' := ih p
712	    have hh : m'.height = p.height + 1 := Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one p m'
713	    have hm_eq : m.height = m'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map e.toRingEquiv m).symm
714	    rw [hm_eq, hh, show ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (n : ℕ∞) + 1 from by push_cast; ring]
715	    gcongr

720	    m.height ≤ Nat.card ιx := by
721	  have h1 : (m.height : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim (MvPolynomial ιx k) :=
722	    Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top hm
723	  rw [MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing, ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_field,
724	    zero_add] at h1
725	  exact_mod_cast h1

727	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card
728	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (n : ℕ)
729	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) [hmax : m.IsMaximal] :
730	    m.height = n := by
731	  apply le_antisymm
732	  · have := MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_le_natCard_of_field k (ιx := Fin n) m hmax.ne_top
733	    simpa using this
734	  · exact MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_ge_card_of_field k n m

736	/-- **Step 2 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), general `[Finite ι]` form.**
737	For a field `k`, a finite index type `ι`, and a maximal ideal
738	`m ⊂ MvPolynomial ι k`, the height of `m` equals `Nat.card ι`.
739	
740	Axiom-clean: transports the `Fin n` form
741	`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card` along the canonical
742	`MvPolynomial.renameEquiv` induced by `(Finite.equivFin ι)`. -/
743	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard.{v}
744	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (ιx : Type v) [Finite ιx]
745	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) [m.IsMaximal] :
746	    m.height = Nat.card ιx := by
747	  classical
748	  haveI : Fintype ιx := Fintype.ofFinite ιx
749	  let σ : ιx ≃ Fin (Fintype.card ιx) := Fintype.equivFin ιx
750	  let e : MvPolynomial ιx k ≃+* MvPolynomial (Fin (Fintype.card ιx)) k :=
751	    (MvPolynomial.renameEquiv k σ).toRingEquiv
752	  let m' : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin (Fintype.card ιx)) k) := Ideal.map e m
753	  haveI : m'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv (e := e)
754	  have hm_eq : m.height = m'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map e m).symm
755	  have hcard : Nat.card ιx = Fintype.card ιx := by simp [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card]
756	  rw [hm_eq, hcard]
757	  exact MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card k (Fintype.card ιx) m'

760	localisation `A` of `MvPolynomial ιx k` at a maximal ideal `m`, the Krull

765	`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard`) and pre-composes with the

767	`ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime`) to expose the closed form
768	`ringKrullDim A = Nat.card ιx` without going through `Ideal.height`.

770	Combined with Step 3 (`ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_of_regular_sequence`)
771	plus the Jacobian-regular-sequence witness (iter-201+ Lane COE), this yields
772	`ringKrullDim Sₘ = Nat.card ιx − rs.length` for the standard-smooth quotient
773	`Sₘ = A ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs`. -/
774	private theorem ringKrullDim_localization_atMaximal_MvPolynomial.{v}
775	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {ιx : Type v} [Finite ιx]
776	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) [m.IsMaximal]
777	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra (MvPolynomial ιx k) A]
778	    [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] :
779	    ringKrullDim A = (Nat.card ιx : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
780	  rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m A,
781	    MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard k ιx m]
782	  rfl

784	/-- **Step 3 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), parameterised additive form.**
785	For a Noetherian local ring `R'` with `ringKrullDim R' = a` (a natural number) and
786	a regular sequence `rs : List R'` of length `b`, the Krull dimension of the
787	quotient `R' ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs` plus `b` equals `a`.
788	
789	This is the parameterised version of Mathlib's
790	`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular` packaged in `WithBot ℕ∞`
791	arithmetic, with the natural-number values of dimension and length explicit. It is
792	the additive form preferred over a subtraction form because `WithBot ℕ∞` does not
793	carry a subtraction structure.
794	
795	The consumer of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` chains:
796	
797	* `R'` = the polynomial-ring localisation `R'_{m'} = (MvPolynomial ι k)_{m'}`,
798	* `hDim` (a = #ι) = `MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard` composed with
799	  the Step 1 bridge `ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime`,
800	* `hReg` = the Jacobian-criterion regular-sequence witness (residual Mathlib gap;
801	  iter-201+ Lane COE),
802	* `hLen` (b = #σ) = the cardinality of the regular sequence.
803	
804	Then `ringKrullDim Sₘ = a − b = n` follows by cancellation from this lemma.

806	Axiom-clean: 3-line re-export. -/
807	private theorem ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_of_regular_sequence
808	    {R' : Type*} [CommRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R'] [IsLocalRing R']
809	    (rs : List R') (hReg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R' rs)
810	    {a b : ℕ} (hDim : ringKrullDim R' = (a : WithBot ℕ∞))
811	    (hLen : rs.length = b) :
812	    ringKrullDim (R' ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (b : WithBot ℕ∞) = (a : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
813	  have h := ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular rs hReg
814	  rw [hDim, hLen] at h
815	  exact h

817	/-! ### Iter-201 Step A2 substrate (cotangent linear-independence transport)
818	
819	Two axiom-clean substrate helpers needed by the iter-201+ A2 step of the
820	Stacks-00SW Jacobian-regular-sequence witness construction
821	(per `analogies/coe-stacks00sw.md`). They package the
822	`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.basisCotangent` linear-independence statement
823	in two forward-friendly forms:
824	
825	* `submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent` — the
826	  `S`-lin-indep version of the family `i ↦ Cotangent.mk ⟨P.relation i, _⟩`,
827	  obtained directly from `P.basisCotangent.linearIndependent` via
828	  `basisCotangent_apply`.
829	* `submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent_localized`
830	  — the same family transported through the canonical localisation map
831	  `Cot →ₗ[S] LocalizedModule p.primeCompl Cot` at any prime ideal `p` of `S`,
832	  yielding `Localization.AtPrime p`-lin-indep. This composes the previous
833	  helper with Mathlib's `LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule`.
834	
835	Together these realise Analogue D of the analogist recipe (lin-indep
836	preservation under localisation). The remaining substantive step in the A2
837	chain — identifying `LocalizedModule p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent`
838	with `(I·A) / (I·A)²` over the localisation `A = S_p`, i.e.\ the
839	conormal-localisation iso (Stacks 02JK, `Algebra.Generators.Cotangent.tensor`
840	variant for `AtPrime` rather than `Localization.Away`) — is a separate
841	Mathlib gap not landed here. -/
842	
843	/-- **Iter-201 Step A2 substrate, Mathlib gap-free part 1.** For a submersive
844	presentation `P : R → S` of an `R`-algebra, the family
845	`i ↦ Cotangent.mk ⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩`
846	in `P.toExtension.Cotangent` is `S`-linearly independent.

64	/-! ## §1. Codim-1 cycle group / Weil divisor group
65	
66	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` (II §6, p. 130) is: `X` is a Noetherian integral
67	separated scheme that is regular in codimension one. Under `(*)`, prime divisors
68	are closed integral subschemes of codimension one, and the local ring at the
69	generic point of a prime divisor is a DVR with quotient field the function field
70	`K(X)`.
71	
72	A prime divisor is encoded by its **generic point** together with the
73	codimension-one witness `Order.coheight point = 1`: the data field `point : X`
74	selects the generic point of the closed integral subscheme, and the predicate
75	field `coheight` enforces codimension one in the specialisation preorder
76	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 130; blueprint pin `def:prime_divisor`). Integrality of the
77	closure is automatic, so no separate integrality witness is needed.
78	-/
79	
80	/-- A **prime divisor** on a scheme `X`: a closed integral subscheme of codimension
81	one, encoded (for a scheme satisfying Hartshorne's condition `(*)`) by its generic
82	point. On a curve, prime divisors correspond bijectively to closed points.
83	
84	The codimension-one witness is the predicate `Order.coheight point = 1` on the
85	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier` (Mathlib's convention is
86	`x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`, so the generic point is the unique maximal element and a
87	generic point of a codim-1 closed integral subscheme has a length-one chain
88	above it). The closure `{point}̄` is automatically irreducible (so the reduced
89	induced subscheme structure is automatically integral); hence no separate
90	integrality field is needed.
91	
92	Blueprint reference: `def:prime_divisor` / `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6
93	p. 130; Stacks 02RW). -/
94	structure Scheme.PrimeDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) where
95	  /-- The generic point of the closed integral subscheme. -/
96	  point : X
97	  /-- Codimension-1 witness: `point` has coheight `1` in the specialisation
98	  preorder on `X.carrier`. Per iter-173 `wd-spec-refine` (`def:prime_divisor`). -/
99	  coheight : Order.coheight point = 1
100	
101	/-- The **Weil divisor group** of a scheme `X` satisfying Hartshorne's condition
102	`(*)`: the free abelian group on the set of prime divisors of `X`,
103	`Div(X) = ⨁_{Y prime divisor of X} ℤ · Y`. An element `D = Σ nᵢ · Yᵢ` with finitely
104	many nonzero coefficients is a **Weil divisor**; if all `nᵢ ≥ 0` it is **effective**.
105	
106	Blueprint reference: `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 130). -/
107	def Scheme.WeilDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u := X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ
108	
109	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
110	
111	noncomputable instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : AddCommGroup X.WeilDivisor :=
112	  inferInstanceAs (AddCommGroup (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
113	
114	instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : Inhabited X.WeilDivisor :=
115	  inferInstanceAs (Inhabited (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
116	
117	end Scheme.WeilDivisor

119	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — PrimeDivisor open-immersion bridge
120	
121	Iter-200 Lane WD-A4a Sub-build 1 substrate (per `analogies/wd-stacks02iz.md`
122	and `task_results/mathlib-analogist-wd-stacks02iz.md`): on top of the existing
123	project-side `AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.CoheightBridge`
124	(`Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding`, axiom-clean iter-183) we package the
125	PrimeDivisor-level open-immersion bijection used by future RR.1 / A.4.a
126	substrate work.
127	
128	The four declarations are:
129	- `Scheme.PrimeDivisor.restrictToOpen` — given a prime divisor `Y` of `X` and
130	  `Y.point ∈ U`, the corresponding prime divisor of the open subscheme `U`.
131	- `Scheme.PrimeDivisor.ofOpen` — push a prime divisor of an open subscheme `U`
132	  back to a prime divisor of the ambient scheme `X`.
133	- `Scheme.PrimeDivisor.equivOpen` — the bijection
134	  `{ Y : X.PrimeDivisor // Y.point ∈ U } ≃ U.toScheme.PrimeDivisor`.
135	- `Scheme.PrimeDivisor.stalkIso` — the stalk identification along the
136	  open immersion `U.ι : U.toScheme ⟶ X`, a thin wrapper around Mathlib's
137	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.stalkIso`.
138	
139	References: Stacks 02IZ (open-immersion stalks), Stacks 005X (coheight ↔ Krull
140	dim on Noetherian schemes); iter-183 substrate at
141	`AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CoheightBridge.lean`.
142	-/
143	
144	namespace Scheme.PrimeDivisor
145	
146	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
147	
148	/-- **Extensionality for `Scheme.PrimeDivisor`.** Two prime divisors with
149	the same underlying point are equal (the coheight witness is a `Prop`-valued
150	field, so it carries no data). Useful for the round-trip lemmas of the
151	`equivOpen` bijection below. -/
152	@[ext]
153	lemma ext {Y Y' : X.PrimeDivisor} (h : Y.point = Y'.point) : Y = Y' := by
154	  cases Y; cases Y'
155	  congr
156	
157	/-- **PrimeDivisor restriction along an open immersion.** Given a prime
158	divisor `Y` of `X` whose generic point lies in an open `U ⊆ X`, the
159	corresponding prime divisor of the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The
160	codimension-one witness transports via `Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding`
161	(iter-183 project substrate, Stacks 02IZ topological side). -/
162	def restrictToOpen (U : X.Opens) (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
163	    (hYU : Y.point ∈ U) : U.toScheme.PrimeDivisor where
164	  point := ⟨Y.point, hYU⟩
165	  coheight := by
166	    rw [← Y.coheight]
167	    exact (Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding U.isOpen Y.point hYU).symm
168	
169	/-- **PrimeDivisor extension from an open subscheme.** A prime divisor of an
170	open subscheme `U.toScheme` lifts to a prime divisor of the ambient `X` along
171	the open immersion `U.ι`. Codimension-one is preserved via the same
172	`Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding` bridge, this time in the forward
173	direction. -/
174	def ofOpen (U : X.Opens) (YU : U.toScheme.PrimeDivisor) :
175	    X.PrimeDivisor where
176	  point := YU.point.1
177	  coheight := by
178	    rw [Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding U.isOpen YU.point.1 YU.point.2]
179	    exact YU.coheight
180	
181	@[simp]
182	lemma restrictToOpen_point (U : X.Opens) (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
183	    (hYU : Y.point ∈ U) :
184	    (restrictToOpen U Y hYU).point = ⟨Y.point, hYU⟩ := rfl
185	
186	@[simp]
187	lemma ofOpen_point (U : X.Opens) (YU : U.toScheme.PrimeDivisor) :
188	    (ofOpen U YU).point = YU.point.1 := rfl
189	
190	/-- **Bijection between prime divisors of `X` lying inside `U` and prime
191	divisors of `U.toScheme`.** The forward map is
192	`Y ↦ Scheme.PrimeDivisor.restrictToOpen U Y.val Y.property`; the inverse is
193	`Scheme.PrimeDivisor.ofOpen U`. Both maps are coheight-preserving by
194	`Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding`. -/
195	def equivOpen (U : X.Opens) :
196	    { Y : X.PrimeDivisor // Y.point ∈ U } ≃ U.toScheme.PrimeDivisor where
197	  toFun Y := restrictToOpen U Y.1 Y.2
198	  invFun YU := ⟨ofOpen U YU, YU.point.2⟩
199	  left_inv := by
200	    rintro ⟨Y, hY⟩
201	    rfl
202	  right_inv := by
203	    intro YU
204	    rfl
205	
206	/-- **Stalk identification along an open immersion at a prime divisor.** A
207	thin wrapper around Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.stalkIso`
208	specialised to the underlying carrier of a prime divisor `Y` lifted from
209	the open subscheme. Stacks 02IZ stalk side. -/
210	noncomputable def stalkIso (U : X.Opens) (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
211	    (hYU : Y.point ∈ U) :
212	    U.toScheme.presheaf.stalk (restrictToOpen U Y hYU).point ≅
213	      X.presheaf.stalk Y.point :=
214	  AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.stalkIso U ⟨Y.point, hYU⟩
215	
216	end Scheme.PrimeDivisor
217	
218	end AlgebraicGeometry

220	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Ring.ordFrac` naturality
221	
222	Iter-201 Lane WD-A4a Sub-build 2 (HARD BAR + extras). Naturality of
223	`Ring.ord`, `Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom`, and `Ring.ordFrac` (Mathlib's
224	`K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` from `Mathlib.RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Basic`) across a ring
225	isomorphism `e : R ≃+* S` between commutative rings, lifted to a compatible
226	fraction-field isomorphism `e_K : Frac R ≃+* Frac S`.
227	
228	This is the algebraic substrate consumed by iter-202+ Sub-build 3
229	(scheme-level `Scheme.RationalMap.order` naturality across the iter-200
230	`Scheme.PrimeDivisor.stalkIso` open-immersion bridge), which in turn closes
231	the non-zero branch of `rationalMap_order_finite_support` (the L535 sorry).
232	
233	References: Stacks 02RV (Hartshorne II.6.1), Stacks 02ME (DVR-of-stalk
234	characterisation at codim-1 points), Stacks 02IZ (stalk under open
235	immersion), Stacks 02MD (Mathlib's `Ring.ordFrac` / `Ring.ord` API).
236	-/
237	
238	namespace Ring
239	
240	/-- **`Ring.ord` naturality under a ring isomorphism.**
241	`ord R x = ord S (e x)`. The length of `R/(x)` as `R`-module equals the
242	length of `S/(e x)` as `S`-module: the `R`-linear equivalence
243	`R/(x) ≃ₗ[R] S/(e x)` (with the `S`-side equipped with `R`-module structure
244	via `e`) transports length across rings using `Module.length_eq_of_surjective`.
245	
246	Project-local because Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `ord` naturality lemma. -/
247	private lemma ord_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
248	    (e : R ≃+* S) (x : R) : Ring.ord R x = Ring.ord S (e x) := by
249	  have heq : Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S) = Ideal.map (e : R →+* S) (Ideal.span {x}) := by
250	    rw [Ideal.map_span, Set.image_singleton]; rfl
251	  letI alg : Algebra R S := e.toRingHom.toAlgebra
252	  haveI : IsScalarTower R S (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) := by
253	    refine ⟨fun r s m => ?_⟩
254	    change (algebraMap R S r * s) • m = (algebraMap R S r) • (s • m)
255	    rw [mul_smul]
256	  let φ_ring : (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≃+* (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) :=
257	    Ideal.quotientEquiv (Ideal.span {x}) (Ideal.span {e x}) e heq
258	  let φ : (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≃ₗ[R] (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) := by
259	    refine { φ_ring.toAddEquiv with map_smul' := ?_ }
260	    intro r m
261	    induction m using Quotient.inductionOn with
262	    | h y =>
263	      change Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (e (r * y)) = e r • Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (e y)
264	      rw [map_mul]; rfl
265	  unfold Ring.ord
266	  rw [φ.length_eq]
267	  exact Module.length_eq_of_surjective (S := R) (R := S) (M := S ⧸ Ideal.span {e x})
268	    e.surjective
269	
270	/-- **`nonZeroDivisors` is preserved by a ring isomorphism.** Direct from
271	Mathlib's `MulEquivClass.map_nonZeroDivisors`. Project-local convenience
272	wrapper to phrase the result as an `Iff` instead of a `Submonoid.map` equality. -/
273	private lemma nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
274	    (e : R ≃+* S) (r : R) :
275	    r ∈ nonZeroDivisors R ↔ e r ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := by
276	  rw [← MulEquivClass.map_nonZeroDivisors (e : R ≃* S)]
277	  refine ⟨fun h => ⟨r, h, rfl⟩, fun h => ?_⟩
278	  obtain ⟨r', hr', heq⟩ := h
279	  have : r = r' := e.injective heq.symm
280	  rw [this]; exact hr'
281	
282	/-- **`Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom` naturality under a ring isomorphism.**
283	`ordMonoidWithZeroHom S (e r) = ordMonoidWithZeroHom R r`. Reduces to
284	`ord_ringEquiv` after splitting on `r ∈ nonZeroDivisors R`. -/
285	private lemma ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [Nontrivial R]
286	    [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] (e : R ≃+* S) (r : R) :
287	    Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom S (e r) = Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom R r := by
288	  unfold Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom
289	  simp only [MonoidWithZeroHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
290	  by_cases hr : r ∈ nonZeroDivisors R
291	  · have her : e r ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := (nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e r).mp hr
292	    simp [hr, her, ord_ringEquiv e r]
293	  · have her : e r ∉ nonZeroDivisors S := fun h =>
294	      hr ((nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e r).mpr h)
295	    simp [hr, her]
296	
297	/-- **`Ring.ordFrac` naturality** — iter-201 Lane WD-A4a Sub-build 2 HARD BAR
298	(per PROGRESS.md L108--L122 recipe step (1)).
299	
300	Given a ring iso `e : R ≃+* S` between Noetherian Krull-dim-`≤ 1` integral
301	domains and a compatible fraction-field iso `e_K : K_R ≃+* K_S` (compatibility
302	`e_K (algebraMap R K_R r) = algebraMap S K_S (e r)` for `r : R`), the
303	order-of-vanishing function on the fraction fields is invariant:
304	`ordFrac S (e_K x) = ordFrac R x` for any `x : K_R`.
305	
306	Proof: `x = 0` is the junk-zero branch. For `x ≠ 0`, write `x = mk' K_R a b`
307	with `a, b ∈ nonZeroDivisors R` via `IsLocalization.surj`. Then
308	`e_K x = mk' K_S (e a) (e b)` via the compatibility hypothesis, and both
309	sides reduce via Mathlib's `ordFrac_eq_div` to a quotient of
310	`ordMonoidWithZeroHom`-values, which agree by `ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv`. -/
311	private lemma ordFrac_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [IsDomain R]
312	    [IsNoetherianRing R] [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 R]
313	    [CommRing S] [IsDomain S] [IsNoetherianRing S] [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 S]
314	    {K_R K_S : Type*} [Field K_R] [Field K_S]
315	    [Algebra R K_R] [IsFractionRing R K_R]
316	    [Algebra S K_S] [IsFractionRing S K_S]
317	    (e : R ≃+* S) (e_K : K_R ≃+* K_S)
318	    (h_compat : ∀ r : R, e_K (algebraMap R K_R r) = algebraMap S K_S (e r))
319	    (x : K_R) :
320	    Ring.ordFrac S (e_K x) = Ring.ordFrac R x := by
321	  by_cases hx : x = 0
322	  · subst hx; rw [map_zero, map_zero, map_zero]
323	  obtain ⟨⟨a, b⟩, hab⟩ := IsLocalization.surj (nonZeroDivisors R) x
324	  have hb_nzd : (b : R) ∈ nonZeroDivisors R := b.2
325	  have hb_ne : (b : R) ≠ 0 := mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_ne_zero.mp hb_nzd
326	  have h_inj_R : Function.Injective (algebraMap R K_R) := IsFractionRing.injective R K_R
327	  have hb_K_ne : algebraMap R K_R (b : R) ≠ 0 := by
328	    rw [Ne, ← map_zero (algebraMap R K_R), h_inj_R.eq_iff]
329	    exact hb_ne
330	  have ha_ne : a ≠ 0 := by
331	    intro ha
332	    subst ha
333	    rw [map_zero] at hab
334	    rcases mul_eq_zero.mp hab with hx' | hb_ne'
335	    · exact hx hx'
336	    · exact hb_K_ne hb_ne'
337	  have ha_nzd : a ∈ nonZeroDivisors R := mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero ha_ne
338	  have he_b_nzd : e b ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := (nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e b).mp hb_nzd
339	  have he_a_nzd : e a ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := (nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e a).mp ha_nzd
340	  have hx_mk : x = IsLocalization.mk' K_R a ⟨b, hb_nzd⟩ := by
341	    rw [eq_comm, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq_mul, hab]
342	  have he_K_x : e_K x = IsLocalization.mk' K_S (e a) ⟨e b, he_b_nzd⟩ := by
343	    rw [eq_comm]
344	    refine (IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq_mul).mpr ?_
345	    rw [← h_compat a, ← h_compat b, ← map_mul]
346	    exact congrArg e_K hab.symm
347	  rw [he_K_x, hx_mk]
348	  rw [Ring.ordFrac_eq_div S ⟨e a, he_a_nzd⟩ ⟨e b, he_b_nzd⟩]
349	  rw [Ring.ordFrac_eq_div R ⟨a, ha_nzd⟩ ⟨b, hb_nzd⟩]
350	  congr 1
351	  · exact ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv e a
352	  · exact ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv e b
353	
354	end Ring

358	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Function field iso for open immersions
359	
360	Iter-201 Lane WD-A4a Sub-build 2 PUSH-BEYOND (step (2) per PROGRESS.md
361	L122--L138): canonical iso `U.toScheme.functionField ≅ X.functionField` for
362	integral `X` and nonempty open `U ⊆ X`, obtained by composing Mathlib's
363	`Scheme.Opens.stalkIso U (genericPoint U.toScheme)` with the transport across
364	the genericPoint equality `(U.ι (genericPoint U.toScheme)) = genericPoint X`
365	delivered by `genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion`.
366	
367	This iso is the bridge that lets `Ring.ordFrac_ringEquiv` apply at the
368	scheme level (with `e_K = Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso U` and `e =
369	Scheme.PrimeDivisor.stalkIso U Y hYU` for a prime divisor `Y` of `X` with
370	`Y.point ∈ U`). The full IsFractionRing-compatibility lemma chaining the
371	two isos is iter-202+ Sub-build 3 scope.
372	
373	Reference: Mathlib `AlgebraicGeometry.FunctionField.genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion`
374	+ iter-200 `Scheme.PrimeDivisor.stalkIso` wrapper. -/
375	
376	/-- **Function field isomorphism along an open immersion** (iter-201 Lane
377	WD-A4a Sub-build 2 PUSH-BEYOND). For an integral scheme `X` and a nonempty
378	open subscheme `U ⊆ X`, the function fields of `U.toScheme` and `X` are
379	canonically isomorphic. -/
380	noncomputable def Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
381	    (U : X.Opens) [Nonempty U] :
382	    (U.toScheme).functionField ≅ X.functionField :=
383	  (Scheme.Opens.stalkIso U (genericPoint U.toScheme)).trans
384	    (X.presheaf.stalkCongr (by
385	      have heq : ((genericPoint U.toScheme : U.toScheme.carrier).val : X.carrier) =
386	          genericPoint X :=
387	        genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion U.ι
388	      rw [heq]
389	      exact Inseparable.refl _))

391	/-! ## §2. Order of a rational function at a prime divisor
392	
393	For a scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, every prime divisor `Y` carries a discrete
394	valuation `v_Y` on the function field `K(X)`. The order of a nonzero rational
395	function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along `Y` is the integer `ord_Y(f) := v_Y(f)`. -/
396	
397	namespace Scheme.RationalMap
398	
399	/-- **Order of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along a prime divisor `Y`.**
400	
401	For `X` satisfying Hartshorne's `(*)`, the local ring `O_{X,η}` at the generic
402	point `η` of `Y` is a discrete valuation ring with quotient field the function
403	field `K(X)`, and `ord_Y(f) = v_Y(f) ∈ ℤ` is the value of the associated normalised
404	discrete valuation on `f`.
405	
406	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
407	divisor and `ord_P(f) = v_P(f)` is the standard DVR valuation at `P`.
408	
409	Blueprint reference: `def:order_at_point` (Hartshorne II §6 pp. 130–131; Stacks 02ME).
410	
411	iter-176 body (per analogist `dvr-rationalmap-order`): the body uses Mathlib's
412	`Ring.ordFrac` (the canonical `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero hom from
413	`Mathlib.RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Basic`, Stacks `02MD`) on the stalk
414	`X.presheaf.stalk Y.point`, then projects through `WithZero.log : ℤᵐ⁰ → ℤ`
415	(the canonical projection with junk-on-zero, `Mathlib.Algebra.GroupWithZero.WithZero`).
416	On `f = 0` this gives `order Y 0 = 0` (junk convention from `WithZero.log_zero`).
417	
418	The required Mathlib typeclasses on the stalk are:
419	- `IsNoetherianRing` — from `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`.
420	- `IsDomain` ⟹ `Nontrivial` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
421	- `IsFractionRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) X.functionField` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
422	- `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` — threaded explicitly (the
423	  topological-coheight-to-Krull-dim bridge `Order.coheight Y.point = 1 ⟹
424	  Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` is a Mathlib-upstream-pending
425	  gap; see Stacks `02IZ` / `005X`). -/
426	noncomputable def order {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
427	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
428	    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]
429	    (f : X.functionField) : ℤ :=
430	  WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) f)
431	
432	end Scheme.RationalMap
433	
434	/-! ### Regular-in-codimension-one bridge class
435	
436	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` includes "regular in codimension one", i.e.\ every
437	prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk `O_{X,Y.point}`. Mathlib has no direct
438	typeclass for this; we package it as the project-bespoke class
439	`Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne`, with an instance synthesising the per-`Y`
440	Krull-dim-≤-1 condition required by `Scheme.RationalMap.order`. Blueprint pin
441	("Iter-173+ may introduce a `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` predicate to
442	abbreviate this"; chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §2 "Standing hypothesis
443	`(*)` in the Lean encoding"). -/
444	
445	/-- Project-bespoke class encoding Hartshorne's "regular in codimension one"
446	clause of `(*)`: every prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk
447	`O_{X,Y.point}`. The `[IsIntegral X]` precondition makes
448	`IsDomain (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` available so that the
449	`IsDiscreteValuationRing` predicate is well-formed
450	(`AlgebraicGeometry.instIsDomainCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafOfIsIntegral`). -/
451	class Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (X : Scheme.{u}) [IsIntegral X] : Prop where
452	  /-- The defining content: every prime divisor's stalk is a discrete valuation
453	  ring. (This is the precise content of Hartshorne's `(*)`; the weaker
454	  `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` derives via the `IsDiscreteValuationRing` ⟹
455	  `IsPrincipalIdealRing` ⟹ `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` chain via the bridge
456	  instance below.) -/
457	  out : ∀ Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X, IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)
458	
459	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
460	synthesis can derive `IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for
461	every prime divisor `Y`. -/
462	instance Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instIsDiscreteValuationRingStalk
463	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
464	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
465	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
466	  Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
467	
468	/-- **Open-immersion descent for `IsRegularInCodimensionOne`** (iter-200 Lane
469	WD-A4a Sub-build 1 PUSH-BEYOND). The codim-1 regularity hypothesis transports
470	along the open immersion `U.ι : U.toScheme ⟶ X`: prime divisors of `U.toScheme`
471	push to prime divisors of `X` via `Scheme.PrimeDivisor.ofOpen`, and Mathlib's
472	`Scheme.Opens.stalkIso` gives the ring iso between stalks. We then transport
473	the `IsDiscreteValuationRing` property via
474	`IsDiscreteValuationRing.RingEquivClass.isDiscreteValuationRing`.
475	
476	References: Stacks 02IZ (open-immersion stalks), iter-183 project substrate
477	in `AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CoheightBridge.lean`. -/
478	instance Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instOpen
479	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
480	    (U : X.Opens) [IsIntegral U.toScheme] :
481	    Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne U.toScheme := by
482	  refine ⟨fun YU => ?_⟩
483	  haveI hY : IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk YU.point.1) :=
484	    Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out (Scheme.PrimeDivisor.ofOpen U YU)
485	  exact IsDiscreteValuationRing.RingEquivClass.isDiscreteValuationRing
486	    (AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.stalkIso U YU.point).symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv
487	
488	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
489	synthesis can derive `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for every
490	prime divisor `Y`, via the `IsDiscreteValuationRing` ⟹ `IsPrincipalIdealRing`
491	⟹ `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` chain (Mathlib's `IsDiscreteValuationRing.toIsPrincipalIdealRing`
492	+ `IsPrincipalIdealRing.krullDimLE_one`). -/
493	instance Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instKrullDimLEStalk
494	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
495	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
496	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
497	  haveI : IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
498	    Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
499	  haveI : IsPrincipalIdealRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
500	    IsDiscreteValuationRing.toIsPrincipalIdealRing
501	  IsPrincipalIdealRing.krullDimLE_one _
502	
503	/-- **Scheme-side packaging of `Ring.ordFrac_ringEquiv` for the open-immersion
504	PrimeDivisor stalk iso** (iter-201 Lane WD-A4a Sub-build 2 PUSH-BEYOND
505	packaging).
506	
507	This is the iter-202+ Sub-build 3 entry point: it pipes the iter-200
508	`Scheme.PrimeDivisor.stalkIso U Y hYU` ring iso plus a user-supplied
509	function-field iso `e_K` (typically the `Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso U`)
510	and a compatibility hypothesis `h_compat` into the algebraic naturality
511	`Ring.ordFrac_ringEquiv`.
512	
513	The full Sub-build 3 closure replaces `e_K` with `Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso U`
514	and discharges `h_compat` from the naturality of `Scheme.Opens.stalkIso` w.r.t.
515	`stalkSpecializes` to the generic point. That naturality lemma is iter-202
516	scope (it requires either a `Scheme.Hom.stalkSpecializes_stalkMap` analogue or a
517	direct germ-universal chase via Mathlib's
518	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.germ_stalkIso_hom_assoc`). -/
519	private theorem Scheme.PrimeDivisor.ordFrac_stalkIso_naturality
520	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
521	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
522	    (U : X.Opens) [Nonempty U] [IsIntegral U.toScheme]
523	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne U.toScheme]
524	    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) (hYU : Y.point ∈ U)
525	    (e_K : U.toScheme.functionField ≃+* X.functionField)
526	    (h_compat : ∀ r : U.toScheme.presheaf.stalk
527	        (Scheme.PrimeDivisor.restrictToOpen U Y hYU).point,
528	      e_K (algebraMap _ U.toScheme.functionField r) =
529	        algebraMap _ X.functionField
530	          ((Scheme.PrimeDivisor.stalkIso U Y hYU).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv r))
531	    (f : U.toScheme.functionField) :
532	    Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) (e_K f) =
533	      Ring.ordFrac (U.toScheme.presheaf.stalk
534	        (Scheme.PrimeDivisor.restrictToOpen U Y hYU).point) f :=
535	  Ring.ordFrac_ringEquiv
536	    (Scheme.PrimeDivisor.stalkIso U Y hYU).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv
537	    e_K h_compat f

539	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Sub-build 3 closure of `h_compat`
540	
541	Iter-202 Lane WD-A4a Sub-build 3 (HARD BAR, both steps). We discharge the
542	`h_compat` hypothesis of `Scheme.PrimeDivisor.ordFrac_stalkIso_naturality`
543	with `e_K := Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso U`, producing the user-facing
544	naturality `Scheme.PrimeDivisor.order_eq_order_restrict`:
545	`order Y (functionFieldIso U f) = order (restrictToOpen U Y hYU) f`.
546	
547	Step 1 is the morphism-level commutativity in `CommRingCat`
548	(`Scheme.PrimeDivisor.functionFieldIso_compat`): the two ways of mapping the
549	stalk of `U.toScheme` at `Y` into `X.functionField` — first specialise to the
550	generic point of `U` then apply `functionFieldIso`, vs. first apply the
551	open-immersion `stalkIso` then specialise to the generic point of `X` —
552	agree. The proof is a germ-chase via `TopCat.Presheaf.stalk_hom_ext`,
553	`germ_stalkSpecializes`, and `Scheme.Opens.germ_stalkIso_hom`.
554	
555	Reference: Stacks 02IZ (open-immersion stalks) + Mathlib's
556	`TopCat.Presheaf.germ_stalkSpecializes` + `Scheme.Opens.germ_stalkIso_hom`.
557	-/
558	
559	/-- **Morphism-level compatibility for the function-field iso** (iter-202 Lane
560	WD-A4a Sub-build 3, step 1). For an integral scheme `X`, a nonempty integral
561	open `U`, and a prime divisor `Y` of `X` with `Y.point ∈ U`, the square
562	```
563	  stalk_U Y  --stalkSpec_U-->  functionField U
564	      |                              |
565	   stalkIso                     functionFieldIso
566	      v                              v
567	  stalk_X Y  --stalkSpec_X-->  functionField X
568	```
569	commutes in `CommRingCat`, where the horizontal maps are the canonical
570	`stalkSpecializes` maps to the respective generic points (i.e. the algebra
571	maps `O_{·,Y} → K(·)`). -/
572	theorem Scheme.PrimeDivisor.functionFieldIso_compat {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
573	    (U : X.Opens) [Nonempty U] [IsIntegral U.toScheme]
574	    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) (hYU : Y.point ∈ U) :
575	    U.toScheme.presheaf.stalkSpecializes
576	        ((genericPoint_spec U.toScheme).specializes (Set.mem_univ
577	          (Scheme.PrimeDivisor.restrictToOpen U Y hYU).point)) ≫
578	        (Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso U).hom =
579	      (Scheme.PrimeDivisor.stalkIso U Y hYU).hom ≫
580	        X.presheaf.stalkSpecializes
581	          ((genericPoint_spec X).specializes (Set.mem_univ Y.point)) := by
582	  apply TopCat.Presheaf.stalk_hom_ext
583	  intro V hxV
584	  have hcongr : ∀ {a b : X} (e : Inseparable a b),
585	      (X.presheaf.stalkCongr e).hom = X.presheaf.stalkSpecializes e.ge := by
586	    intros; rfl
587	  simp only [Scheme.PrimeDivisor.stalkIso, Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso, Iso.trans_hom,
588	    restrictToOpen_point, hcongr]
589	  simp only [TopCat.Presheaf.germ_stalkSpecializes_assoc,
590	    Scheme.Opens.germ_stalkIso_hom_assoc]
591	  exact (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_stalkSpecializes _ _ _).trans
592	    (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_stalkSpecializes _ _ _).symm
593	
594	/-- **Naturality of `Scheme.RationalMap.order` across the function-field iso**
595	(iter-202 Lane WD-A4a Sub-build 3, step 2 — the HARD BAR endpoint). For an
596	integral locally-Noetherian scheme `X` regular in codimension one, a nonempty
597	integral open `U`, a prime divisor `Y` of `X` with `Y.point ∈ U`, and a
598	rational function `f` of `U.toScheme`, the order of the transported function
599	`functionFieldIso U f` along `Y` equals the order of `f` along the restricted
600	prime divisor `restrictToOpen U Y hYU`. This is the user-facing closure
601	consumer of Sub-build 2's `ordFrac_stalkIso_naturality`, with `h_compat`
602	discharged by the morphism-level `functionFieldIso_compat`. -/
603	theorem Scheme.PrimeDivisor.order_eq_order_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
604	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
605	    (U : X.Opens) [Nonempty U] [IsIntegral U.toScheme]
606	    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) (hYU : Y.point ∈ U)
607	    (f : U.toScheme.functionField) :
608	    Scheme.RationalMap.order Y
609	        ((Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso U).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv f) =
610	      Scheme.RationalMap.order (Scheme.PrimeDivisor.restrictToOpen U Y hYU) f := by
611	  have hcompat : ∀ r : U.toScheme.presheaf.stalk
612	      (Scheme.PrimeDivisor.restrictToOpen U Y hYU).point,
613	      (Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso U).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv
614	          (algebraMap _ U.toScheme.functionField r) =
615	        algebraMap _ X.functionField
616	          ((Scheme.PrimeDivisor.stalkIso U Y hYU).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv r) := by
617	    intro r
618	    have hM := Scheme.PrimeDivisor.functionFieldIso_compat U Y hYU
619	    have happ := congrArg (fun φ => (CommRingCat.Hom.hom φ) r) hM
620	    simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply] at happ
621	    exact happ
622	  unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
623	  rw [Scheme.PrimeDivisor.ordFrac_stalkIso_naturality U Y hYU
624	      (Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso U).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv hcompat f]

626	/-! ### Order-on-curve algebraic identities
627	
628	iter-198 §2 substrate lemmas (Lane WD-A4a PUSH-BEYOND). These are
629	axiom-clean per-prime-divisor algebraic identities on

837	/-! ## §3. Divisor of a closed point on a curve
838	
839	On a smooth proper curve `C` over a field, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
840	divisor (it is closed, integral, of codimension one in the one-dimensional integral
841	scheme `C`). The associated Weil divisor is `[P] = 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`. -/
842	
843	/-- **The Weil divisor associated to a closed point `P` on a curve.** The element
844	`[P] := 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`, i.e. the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one.
845	
846	For a smooth proper curve every closed point is automatically a prime divisor (it
847	is a codimension-one integral closed subscheme of the one-dimensional integral
848	scheme `C`); conversely every prime divisor on a curve is of this form, so an
849	arbitrary divisor on `C` is a finite formal `ℤ`-linear combination
850	`Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` of closed points.
851	
852	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_closed_point` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137).
853	
854	iter-174 body: the function is junk-defined outside its intended scope. We
855	case-split on `Order.coheight P = 1` (the codimension-one witness of
856	`PrimeDivisor`). On the branch where the equality holds — automatic for a
857	closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme — we promote `P` to a
858	`PrimeDivisor` via the witness and return `Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1`, i.e.
859	the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one. On the off-branch (junk regime)
860	we return the zero divisor. The blueprint pins the well-definedness argument
861	"`IsClosed {P}` on a one-dimensional integral scheme ⟹ `coheight P = 1`" as a
862	separate threadable hypothesis at the call site (chapter L330–L340 "Lean
863	signature scope"); see `ofClosedPoint_eq_single` for the equation in the
864	hypothesised regime. -/
865	noncomputable def ofClosedPoint {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
866	    (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) : C.WeilDivisor :=
867	  if h : Order.coheight P = 1 then Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 else 0
868	
869	/-- In the hypothesised regime where the closed point `P` has coheight one
870	(the codim-1 condition automatic for a closed point of a one-dimensional
871	integral scheme), `ofClosedPoint P hP` is the prime divisor `P` with
872	multiplicity one. -/
873	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_single {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
874	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P = 1) :
875	    ofClosedPoint P hP = Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 := by
876	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
877	
878	/-- Off-branch: outside the codim-1 regime, `ofClosedPoint` is junk-defined as
879	the zero divisor. (Only relevant when the user supplies a "closed point" that
880	does not have coheight one in the ambient scheme — e.g. a generic point or a
881	codim-≥2 point on a higher-dimensional scheme.) -/
882	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_zero {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
883	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P ≠ 1) :
884	    ofClosedPoint P hP = 0 := by
885	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]

887	/-! ## §4. Degree of a divisor over an algebraically closed base -/
888	
889	/-- **Degree of a Weil divisor on a smooth proper curve over `k̄`.**
890	
891	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every closed point of a smooth proper
892	curve `C` has residue field `k̄`, so each prime divisor `[P]` contributes degree
893	one to the sum, and `deg(D) := Σᵢ nᵢ` is the sum of the integer coefficients of
894	the formal sum `D = Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]`.
895	
896	(Over a general field `k` one weights by the residue-field degrees
897	`Σᵢ nᵢ · [κ(Pᵢ) : k]` to recover the geometric degree; the project's RR bridge
898	needs only the `k̄` specialisation.)
899	
900	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137; Stacks 0BE0).
901	
902	Implementation: `Finsupp.sum D (fun _ n => n)` is the sum of all coefficients of
903	the finitely supported function representing `D`. -/
904	noncomputable def degree (D : X.WeilDivisor) : ℤ :=
905	  (D : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ).sum (fun _ n => n)
906	
907	/-- **The degree map is a group homomorphism `Div(C) → ℤ`.**
908	
909	`deg(D₁ + D₂) = deg(D₁) + deg(D₂)`, `deg(-D) = -deg(D)`, `deg(0) = 0`. Bundled
910	as an `AddMonoidHom` for downstream use (the linear-equivalence quotient
911	`Cl(C) := Div(C) / im(div)` will inherit a `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ` from this).
912	
913	Blueprint reference: `thm:divisor_degree_hom` (immediate from `def:divisor_degree`).
914	
915	Implementation: built from `Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)`, the
916	generic Mathlib packaging that lifts a family of `AddMonoidHom`s indexed by the
917	support into a single `AddMonoidHom` on the finsupp. Unfolds to
918	`D.sum (fun _ z ↦ z) = degree D` (see `degree_hom_apply` below). -/
919	noncomputable def degree_hom : X.WeilDivisor →+ ℤ :=
920	  Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)
921	
922	@[simp]
923	lemma degree_hom_apply (D : X.WeilDivisor) : degree_hom D = degree D :=
924	  Finsupp.liftAddHom_apply (α := X.PrimeDivisor) (M := ℤ) (N := ℤ)
925	    (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ) D
926	
927	/-- **Degree of the zero divisor is zero.** A direct consequence of
928	`degree_hom` being an `AddMonoidHom`. -/
929	@[simp]
930	lemma degree_zero : degree (0 : X.WeilDivisor) = 0 := by
931	  rw [← degree_hom_apply]; exact map_zero _
932	
933	/-- **Degree is additive.** A direct consequence of `degree_hom` being an
934	`AddMonoidHom`. -/
935	lemma degree_add (D₁ D₂ : X.WeilDivisor) :
936	    degree (D₁ + D₂) = degree D₁ + degree D₂ := by
937	  rw [← degree_hom_apply, ← degree_hom_apply, ← degree_hom_apply]
938	  exact map_add _ _ _
939	
940	/-- **Degree of the negation.** `deg(-D) = -deg D`. A direct consequence of
941	`degree_hom` being an `AddMonoidHom`.
942	
943	iter-198 §4 substrate sharpening (Lane WD-A4a PUSH-BEYOND). -/
944	@[simp]
945	lemma degree_neg (D : X.WeilDivisor) :
946	    degree (-D) = -degree D := by
947	  rw [← degree_hom_apply, ← degree_hom_apply]
948	  exact map_neg _ _
949	
950	/-- **Degree is subtractive.** `deg(D₁ - D₂) = deg D₁ - deg D₂`. A direct
951	consequence of `degree_hom` being an `AddMonoidHom`.
952	
953	iter-198 §4 substrate sharpening (Lane WD-A4a PUSH-BEYOND). -/
954	lemma degree_sub (D₁ D₂ : X.WeilDivisor) :
955	    degree (D₁ - D₂) = degree D₁ - degree D₂ := by
956	  rw [← degree_hom_apply, ← degree_hom_apply, ← degree_hom_apply]
957	  exact map_sub _ _ _

959	/-! ## §5. Principal divisors -/
960	
961	/-- **The principal divisor of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×`.**
962	
963	By Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, `ord_Y(f) = 0` for all but finitely many prime
964	divisors `Y`, so the formal sum
965	`div(f) := Σ_{Y prime divisor} ord_Y(f) · Y ∈ Div(X)`
966	has finite support and is a well-defined Weil divisor.
967	
968	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, this specialises to
969	`div(f) = Σ_{P closed point} ord_P(f) · [P]`.
970	
971	Blueprint reference: `def:principal_divisor` (Hartshorne II §6 Lemma 6.1 +
972	following definition, p. 131).
973	
974	iter-177 body: the construction uses `Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` with the
975	finite-support witness `rationalMap_order_finite_support`. The latter is a
976	private theorem packaging Hartshorne 6.1; its body is a Mathlib-pending gap
977	(see chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5 sub-build note) and is left as
978	a `sorry` for an iter-178+ Mathlib-upstream PR. -/
979	noncomputable def principal [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
980	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField)
981	    (_hf : f ≠ 0) : X.WeilDivisor :=
982	  Finsupp.ofSupportFinite
983	    (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)
984	    (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)
985	
986	/-- **The coefficient of `principal f hf` at a prime divisor `Y` is the order of
987	`f` along `Y`.** This is the basic structural unfolding of the
988	`Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` packaging in `principal`; one-line via
989	`Finsupp.ofSupportFinite_coe` from `Mathlib.Data.Finsupp.Defs`.
990	
991	iter-193 substrate helper for the Lane I body close
992	(`degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`). -/
993	lemma principal_apply [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
994	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0)
995	    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) :
996	    (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf) Y =
997	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f := by
998	  change (Finsupp.ofSupportFinite
999	      (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)
1000	      (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)) Y = _
1001	  rw [Finsupp.ofSupportFinite_coe]

1003	/-- **`Scheme.RationalMap.order Y 1 = 0`** — the order of the constant

1007	iter-193 substrate helper for further structural results

1009	@[simp]
1010	lemma _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order_one
1011	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
1012	    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
1013	    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)] :
1014	    Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (1 : X.functionField) = 0 := by
1015	  unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
1016	  rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]

1240	    have hfx : f x = 0 := hx
1241	    rw [hf_eval] at hfx
1242	    -- Apply `(1 : κ) ⊗_R -` to `Σ x i • m i = 0`.

1244	      rw [hfx]; exact TensorProduct.tmul_zero _ _
1245	    rw [TensorProduct.tmul_sum] at h1

1247	    have hrewrite : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i)
1248	        = (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i := by
1249	      intro i
1250	      rw [show ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
1251	          = x i • ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i) from
1252	        (TensorProduct.tmul_smul (R := R) (x i) (1 : κ) (m i))]
1253	      rw [hm i]; rfl
1254	    rw [show (∑ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
1255	        = ∑ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i from
1256	      Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => hrewrite i)] at h1
1257	    -- Linear independence of `b` forces each `residue (x i) = 0`.
1258	    have hlin : LinearIndependent κ b := b.linearIndependent
1259	    have hall : ∀ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) = 0 := by
1260	      have := Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hlin
1261	        (fun i => IsLocalRing.residue R (x i)) h1
1262	      exact fun i => this i

1264	    have hx_pi : ∀ i, x i ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
1265	      intro i

1267	      rwa [IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff] at this

1270	    intro i _
1271	    have hsingle :
1272	        (Pi.single i (x i) : Fin n → R)
1273	          = (x i) • (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R) := by
1274	      ext j; by_cases hij : i = j <;> simp [Pi.single, Function.update, hij]

1353	
1354	For a surjection `f : R^n →ₗ M` from a finite free module of rank `n ≥ 1` over a
1355	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, with both `M` and `ker f` nontrivial, the depth
1356	of `ker f` admits the lower bound
1357	```
1358	  min(depth_𝔪 R, depth_𝔪 M + 1) ≤ depth_𝔪 (ker f).
1359	```
1360	This is Stacks 00LE (3) (`depth_of_short_exact` part 3) applied to the
1361	SES `0 → ker f → R^n → M → 0`, combined with `depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty`
1362	to identify `depth(R^n)` with `depth(R)`.
1363	
1364	This is the ≥ direction of the LES analysis on the SES; the matching ≤ direction
1365	(`depth(ker f) ≤ min(depth R, depth M + 1)`) is the residual gap needed for the
1366	inductive step of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula. It requires either the "what
1367	is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) or a refined LES argument when
1368	`depth M < depth R`; the current substrate provides only the easy direction. -/
1369	lemma depth_ker_ge_min_of_surjection_finite_localRing
1370	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1371	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] [Nontrivial M]
1372	    {n : ℕ} (hn : 1 ≤ n) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M) (hf : Function.Surjective f)
1373	    [Nontrivial (LinearMap.ker f)] :
1374	    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R)
1375	        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M + 1)
1376	      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (LinearMap.ker f) := by
1377	  -- Set up instances on the auxiliary modules.
1378	  haveI : Inhabited (Fin n) := ⟨⟨0, hn⟩⟩
1379	  haveI : Nonempty (Fin n) := ⟨default⟩
1380	  haveI : Nontrivial (Fin n → R) := Pi.nontrivial
1381	  haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (LinearMap.ker f) := Module.IsNoetherian.finite R _
1382	  -- The SES: 0 → ker f → R^n → M → 0.
1383	  have hex : Function.Exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f :=
1384	    LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map f
1385	  have hinj : Function.Injective (LinearMap.ker f).subtype :=
1386	    Subtype.val_injective
1387	  -- Apply `depth_of_short_exact` part (3).
1388	  have htriple := depth_of_short_exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f hinj hf hex
1389	  have h3 := htriple.2.2
1390	  -- Rewrite `depth (Fin n → R) = depth R`.
1391	  have heq : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (Fin n → R)
1392	      = depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R :=
1393	    depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty _
1394	  rw [heq] at h3
1395	  exact h3

1408	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] [Nontrivial M]
1409	    {n : ℕ} (hn : 1 ≤ n) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M) (hf : Function.Surjective f)
1410	    [Nontrivial (LinearMap.ker f)] :
1411	    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R)
1412	        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (LinearMap.ker f) - 1)
1413	      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1414	    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R)
1415	          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M + 1)
1416	      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (LinearMap.ker f) := by
1417	  haveI : Inhabited (Fin n) := ⟨⟨0, hn⟩⟩
1418	  haveI : Nonempty (Fin n) := ⟨default⟩
1419	  haveI : Nontrivial (Fin n → R) := Pi.nontrivial
1420	  haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (LinearMap.ker f) := Module.IsNoetherian.finite R _
1421	  have hex : Function.Exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f :=
1422	    LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map f

1426	  have heq : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (Fin n → R)
1427	      = depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R :=

1430	  · have h2 := htriple.2.1

1435	/-! ### Helper iter-202 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): nonzero `Ext` at the depth
1436	index.
1437	
1438	The converse read-off of `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`: for a nonzero finite
1439	module `M` of depth exactly `↑D` over a Noetherian local ring, there is a
1440	nonzero element of `Ext^D_R(κ, M)`. (Below `↑D` all `Ext` vanish; the strict
1441	failure of vanishing at `↑(D+1)` must therefore occur at index `D`.) This is
1442	the input that exhibits a nonzero class in the base case of Auslander–Buchsbaum:
1443	`Ext^{depth R}(κ, R^k) ≠ 0`. -/
1444	lemma exists_ne_zero_ext_of_depth_eq
1445	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1446	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1447	    [Nontrivial M] {D : ℕ}
1448	    (hD : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M = (D : ℕ∞)) :
1449	    ∃ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
1450	        (ModuleCat.of R M) D, e ≠ 0 := by
1451	  -- Below `D`, all Ext vanish.
1452	  have hvanish : ∀ i : ℕ, i < D → ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
1453	      (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)) (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 :=
1454	    (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) D).mp (by rw [hD])
1455	  -- `↑(D+1) ≤ depth M` fails, so vanishing below `D+1` fails.
1456	  have hnle : ¬ ((D + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
1457	    rw [hD, Nat.cast_le]; omega
1458	  rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (D + 1)] at hnle
1459	  push_neg at hnle
1460	  obtain ⟨i, hi, e, he⟩ := hnle
1461	  have hiD : i = D := by
1462	    by_contra hne
1463	    have : i < D := by omega
1464	    exact he (hvanish i this e)
1465	  subst hiD
1466	  exact ⟨e, he⟩
