1468	/-! ### Helper iter-201 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): matrix decomposition and
1469	matrix-collapse on `Ext`.
1470	
1471	For an R-linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n` between standard free modules over a
1472	commutative ring R, A decomposes as `A = ∑_{(i,j)} A_{i,j} • E_{i,j}` where
1473	`A_{i,j} = (A (Pi.single j 1)) i` is the matrix entry and `E_{i,j}` is the
1474	"elementary" linear map sending `Pi.single j 1 ↦ Pi.single i 1`. From this
1475	decomposition combined with R-bilinearity of `Ext.comp` and the axiom-clean
1476	helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`, we obtain the matrix-collapse
1477	result: if every entry of A lies in `Ann_R N`, then the induced postcomposition
1478	`Ext^p(N, R^m) → Ext^p(N, R^n)` via `mk₀ (ofHom A)` is the zero map.
1479	
1480	This is the key new substrate for closing the **Path B base case `pd M = 1`**
1481	of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula: given a minimal surjection `f : R^n ↠ M`
1482	with `ker f` free of positive rank, the inclusion `ker f ≅ R^k ↪ R^n` is an
1483	R-linear map with entries in 𝔪 (by minimality `ker f ≤ 𝔪 • ⊤`); the
1484	matrix-collapse then forces the LES injectivity needed to conclude
1485	`depth M < depth R`. -/
1486	
1487	/-- The "elementary matrix" linear map `E_{i,j} : R^m →ₗ R^n` sending
1488	`Pi.single j 1 ↦ Pi.single i 1` and all other standard basis vectors to 0. -/
1489	private def elemMap {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) :
1490	    (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R) :=
1491	  (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R) (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R)) ∘ₗ
1492	    (LinearMap.proj (R := R) (φ := fun _ : Fin m => R) j)
1493	
1494	/-- The elementary map `E_{i,j}` evaluated at `x : R^m` gives `Pi.single i (x j)`. -/
1495	private lemma elemMap_apply {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ)
1496	    (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) (x : Fin m → R) :
1497	    (elemMap n m i j : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) x = Pi.single i (x j) := by
1498	  classical
1499	  change (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R)
1500	    (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R)) (x j) = Pi.single i (x j)
1501	  rw [LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply]
1502	  ext k
1503	  rw [Pi.smul_apply]
1504	  by_cases hk : k = i
1505	  · subst hk; rw [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
1506	  · rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, smul_zero]
1507	
1508	/-- Matrix decomposition: every R-linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n` can be written
1509	as a sum `∑_{(i,j)} A (Pi.single j 1) i • elemMap n m i j` of elementary maps
1510	weighted by matrix entries. -/
1511	private lemma linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
1512	    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) :
1513	    A = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m, (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2 := by
1514	  classical
1515	  refine LinearMap.ext fun (x : Fin m → R) => ?_
1516	  rw [LinearMap.sum_apply]
1517	  rw [show A x = ∑ j : Fin m, ∑ i : Fin n,
1518	      (A (Pi.single j 1) i) • (Pi.single i (x j) : Fin n → R) from ?_]
1519	  · rw [← Finset.univ_product_univ, Finset.sum_product_right]
1520	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
1521	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun i _ => ?_
1522	    rw [LinearMap.smul_apply, elemMap_apply]
1523	  · have hx_decomp : x = ∑ j : Fin m, (x j) • (Pi.single j (1 : R) : Fin m → R) := by
1524	      ext k
1525	      rw [Finset.sum_apply, Finset.sum_eq_single k]
1526	      · simp
1527	      · intro b _ hb
1528	        rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hb.symm, smul_zero]
1529	      · intro h; exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h
1530	    conv_lhs => rw [hx_decomp, map_sum]
1531	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
1532	    rw [map_smul]
1533	    ext k
1534	    rw [Pi.smul_apply, Finset.sum_apply]
1535	    rw [Finset.sum_eq_single k]
1536	    · rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, smul_eq_mul, mul_comm]
1537	    · intro b _ hb
1538	      rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne (Ne.symm hb), smul_zero]
1539	    · intro h; exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h
1540	
1541	/-- **Matrix-collapse on Ext.** For an R-linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n` whose every
1542	matrix entry `A (Pi.single j 1) i` lies in `Ann_R N`, the postcomposition map
1543	`Ext^p(N, R^m) → Ext^p(N, R^n)` induced by `mk₀ (ofHom A)` is the zero map.
1544	
1545	Proof: write `A = ∑_{(i,j)} A_{i,j} • E_{i,j}` via `linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp`.
1546	Push through `ofHom`, `mk₀`, and `Ext.comp` using `ofHom_sum / mk₀_sum / comp_sum`
1547	plus `ofHom_smul / mk₀_smul / comp_smul`. Each summand becomes
1548	`A_{i,j} • (e.comp (mk₀ (ofHom (elemMap _ _ i j))))`, where the scalar `A_{i,j}`
1549	lies in `Ann_R N`. The existing `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` (Stacks
1550	00LP fragment) makes each such scalar action zero. Hence the total sum is zero. -/
1551	private lemma ext_comp_mk₀_ofHom_eq_zero_of_entries_mem_annihilator
1552	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
1553	    {N : ModuleCat.{u} R}
1554	    {n m : ℕ}
1555	    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R))
1556	    (hA : ∀ (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m),
1557	        A (Pi.single j 1) i ∈ _root_.Module.annihilator R (N : Type u))
1558	    {p : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} N (ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R)) p) :
1559	    e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀
1560	              (ModuleCat.ofHom A :
1561	                ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R)))
1562	          (add_zero p) = 0 := by
1563	  classical
1564	  rw [linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp A]
1565	  rw [show (ModuleCat.ofHom (∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
1566	      A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) :
1567	      ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R))
1568	      = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m, ModuleCat.ofHom
1569	          (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) from by
1570	    refine ModuleCat.hom_ext ?_
1571	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum]
1572	    rfl]
1573	  rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_sum]
1574	  rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.comp_sum]
1575	  apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
1576	  intro ij _
1577	  rw [show (ModuleCat.ofHom (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) :
1578	      ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R))
1579	      = A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • ModuleCat.ofHom (elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) from by
1580	    refine ModuleCat.hom_ext ?_; rfl]
1581	  rw [show (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀
1582	      (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • ModuleCat.ofHom (elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2)) :
1583	        Abelian.Ext.{u} (ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R)) (ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R)) 0)

1585	          (ModuleCat.ofHom (elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2)) from
1586	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul (R := R) _ _]

1592	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
1593	
1594	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
1595	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
1596	```
1597	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
1598	```
1599	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
1600	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
1601	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
1602	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
1603	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
1604	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
1605	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
1606	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
1607	
1608	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/
1609	
1610	/-! ### Helper iter-202 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): ℕ∞ combine for the
1611	inductive step of Auslander–Buchsbaum.
1612	
1613	Pure arithmetic in `ℕ∞ = WithTop ℕ`: packages the inductive hypothesis
1614	`j + depth(K) = depth(R)` together with the two `depth_of_short_exact`
1615	inequalities (parts (2) and (3)) on the SES `0 → K → R^n → M → 0` — after
1616	identifying `depth(R^n) = depth(R)` — into the conclusion
1617	`(j+1) + depth(M) = depth(R)`. Valid for `j ≥ 1` (the inductive step
1618	`pd M ≥ 2`); the `j = 0` / `pd M = 1` base case is handled separately via
1619	matrix-collapse since part (3) is then vacuous. -/
1620	private lemma enat_ab_inductive_combine {j : ℕ} {d dK dM : ℕ∞}
1621	    (hIH : (j : ℕ∞) + dK = d)
1622	    (h2 : min d (dK - 1) ≤ dM)
1623	    (h3 : min d (dM + 1) ≤ dK)
1624	    (hj : 1 ≤ j) :
1625	    ((j + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) + dM = d := by
1626	  subst hIH
1627	  cases dK with
1628	  | top =>
1629	    -- `dK = ⊤`: part (2) forces `dM = ⊤`, both sides are `⊤`.
1630	    have hdM : dM = ⊤ := by
1631	      have : (⊤ : ℕ∞) ≤ dM := by simpa using h2
1632	      simpa using top_le_iff.mp this
1633	    subst hdM; simp
1634	  | coe K =>
1635	    cases dM with
1636	    | top =>
1637	      -- `dM = ⊤`: part (3) gives `↑(j+K) ≤ ↑K`, i.e. `j ≤ 0`, contradiction.
1638	      exfalso
1639	      have : (j : ℕ∞) + (K : ℕ∞) ≤ (K : ℕ∞) := by simpa using h3
1640	      rw [← Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_le] at this
1641	      omega
1642	    | coe m =>
1643	      -- All finite: reduce parts (2),(3) to `ℕ` disjunctions and finish with `omega`.
1644	      have h2' : min ((j : ℕ∞) + (K : ℕ∞)) ((K : ℕ∞) - 1) ≤ (m : ℕ∞) := h2
1645	      have h3' : min ((j : ℕ∞) + (K : ℕ∞)) ((m : ℕ∞) + 1) ≤ (K : ℕ∞) := h3
1646	      rw [show ((K : ℕ∞) - 1) = ((K - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) by
1647	            cases K with
1648	            | zero => simp
1649	            | succ K => push_cast; rfl,
1650	          ← Nat.cast_add] at h2'
1651	      rw [show ((m : ℕ∞) + 1) = ((m + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) by push_cast; rfl,
1652	          ← Nat.cast_add] at h3'
1653	      simp only [min_le_iff, Nat.cast_le] at h2' h3'
1654	      rw [← Nat.cast_add, ← Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_inj]
1655	      omega
1656	
1657	/-! ### Helper iter-202 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): exact projective dimension
1658	of the syzygy `ker f`.
1659	
1660	For a surjection `f : R^n ↠ M` with `pd_R M = k+2`, the kernel satisfies
1661	`pd_R (ker f) = k+1` exactly. The upper bound `≤ k+1` is the syzygy-descent
1662	helper `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection` packaged through
1663	`projectiveDimension_le_iff`; the lower bound `≥ k+1` is the contrapositive of
1664	the ascent helper `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker`
1665	through `projectiveDimension_ge_iff` (if `pd(ker f) < k+1` then `pd M < k+2`,
1666	contradiction). This is the exact-pd input the inductive step needs to invoke
1667	its induction hypothesis on `ker f`. -/
1668	private lemma projectiveDimension_ker_eq_of_surjection
1669	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
1670	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
1671	    {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M) (hf : Function.Surjective f)
1672	    {k : ℕ}
1673	    (hpdM : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M = ((k + 2 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)) :
1674	    _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f) = ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
1675	  have hM3 : HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ModuleCat.of R M) (k + 2 + 1) :=
1676	    Module.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_projectiveDimension_eq hpdM
1677	  have hK2 : HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ModuleCat.of R (LinearMap.ker f)) (k + 2) :=
1678	    Module.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection f hf (k := k + 1) hM3
1679	  have hle : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f)
1680	      ≤ ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
1681	    rw [show _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f)
1682	          = CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R (LinearMap.ker f)) from rfl,
1683	        CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_le_iff]
1684	    exact hK2
1685	  have hge : ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)
1686	      ≤ _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f) := by
1687	    rw [show _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f)
1688	          = CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R (LinearMap.ker f)) from rfl,
1689	        CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_ge_iff]
1690	    intro hK1
1691	    have hM2 : HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ModuleCat.of R M) (k + 2) :=
1692	      Module.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker f hf (k := k) hK1
1693	    have hlt : CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M)
1694	        < ((k + 2 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) :=
1695	      CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_lt_iff.mpr hM2
1696	    rw [show CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M)
1697	          = _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M from rfl, hpdM] at hlt
1698	    exact absurd hlt (lt_irrefl _)
1699	  exact le_antisymm hle hge

1044	stalk of `X` at every point is a regular local ring.

1284	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1285	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
1286	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1287	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1288	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1289	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
1290	    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) ∧
1291	      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
1292	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk (needed for the cotangent-space API):
1293	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the automatic `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
1294	  -- through to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk picks up
1295	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance).
1296	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
1297	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
1298	  -- Regularity half: extracted to the named helper above
1299	  -- (`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`), which captures the Stacks-00TT
1300	  -- Jacobian-criterion gap as a narrow named typed sorry. No further
1301	  -- inline `sorry` is required at this step.
1302	  have hreg : IsRegularLocalRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
1303	    isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth X z
1304	  -- Krull-dim half: closes via the iter-183 axiom-clean CoheightBridge
1305	  -- equality + the codim-1 witness `_hz`.
1306	  have hdim : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1 := by
1307	    rw [Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight]
1308	    exact_mod_cast _hz

1310	  -- (Mathlib `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).

1312	      Module.finrank
1313	        (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z))
1314	        (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) = 1 := by
1315	    have h := (IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace _).mp hreg

1321	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) :=

1339	`Order.coheight η = 1`, the stalk `X.left.presheaf.stalk η` is a discrete
1340	valuation ring (with fraction field the function field `K(X)`).

1343	(Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing.isDiscreteValuationRing_iff` or analogous)
1344	plus the smooth-variety fact that every local ring at a codim-1 point is
1345	regular of dimension `1`. The geometric step is packaged in the private helper
1346	`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`; the rest assembles the
1347	DVR instance via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE`.
1348	
1349	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130;
1350	Stacks `00PD` for the regular-of-dim-1 ⇔ DVR equivalence). -/
1351	theorem localRing_dvr_of_codim_one
1352	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1353	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1354	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1355	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1356	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1357	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
1358	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
1359	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:
1360	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the (automatic) `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
1361	  -- to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk inherits
1362	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance). The stalk of an integral scheme is
1363	  -- a domain (Mathlib instance), and stalks of schemes are always local (Mathlib
1364	  -- instance); these are picked up automatically by typeclass search.

1384	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
1385	
1386	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
1387	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
1388	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
1389	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
1390	
1391	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
1392	
1393	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
1394	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
1395	
1396	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
1397	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
1398	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
1399	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
1400	
1401	The proof combines two steps:
1402	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
1403	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
1404	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
1405	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
1406	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
1407	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
1408	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
1409	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
1410	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
1411	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
1412	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
1413	
1414	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1415	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
1416	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
1417	
1418	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
1419	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
1420	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
1421	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1422	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1423	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1424	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1425	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1426	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1427	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
1428	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1429	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
1430	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
1431	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
1432	  -- Derived structural instance: the target Y.left is a separated scheme
1433	  -- (as `Y.hom : Y.left ⟶ Spec kbar` is separated and `Spec kbar` is affine
1434	  -- hence separated over the terminal).
1435	  haveI : Y.left.IsSeparated := by
1436	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
1437	    have heq : terminal.from Y.left = Y.hom ≫ terminal.from _ := Subsingleton.elim _ _
1438	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
1439	  -- The substantive Milne 3.1 proof body reduces to showing Dom(f) = ⊤:
1440	  --
1441	  -- * Step 1 (codim-1 components of Z(f) ruled out) uses the valuative
1442	  --   criterion of properness on the complete target Y plus the DVR-stalk
1443	  --   structure at codim-1 points (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` above, gated
1444	  --   on `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6 Mathlib gap).
1445	  -- * Step 2 (codim-≥2 extension) uses the depth-≥2 local-cohomology
1446	  --   vanishing `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point (Stacks 0AVF), a
1447	  --   genuine Mathlib gap at commit `b80f227`. The codim-1-free hypothesis
1448	  --   `_hf` alone suffices for Step 1's conclusion but Step 2 requires the
1449	  --   local-cohomology / `Module.depth` bridge from
1450	  --   `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`).
1451	  --
1452	  -- Once `Dom(f) = ⊤` is established, the unique morphism extension is the
1453	  -- composition `X.left.topIso.inv ≫ (X.left.isoOfEq h).inv ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom`;
1454	  -- uniqueness follows from the reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1455	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` / `PartialMap.equiv_iff_of_isSeparated`).
1456	  --
1457	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: this lane is gated on Stacks 0AVF
1458	  -- (depth-≥2 H¹ vanishing) + `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6.
1459	  sorry

1461	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
1462	
1463	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
1464	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
1465	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
1466	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
1467	
1468	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
1469	
1470	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
1471	codim 1.**
1472	
1473	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1474	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1475	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1476	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
1477	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
1478	
1479	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1480	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1481	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1482	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1483	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1484	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1485	
1486	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
1487	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
1488	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
1489	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1490	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1491	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1492	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1493	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1494	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1495	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
1496	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
1497	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
1498	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
1499	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
1500	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1501	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) := by
1502	  -- Milne's 4-substep proof body — see informal/milne-lemma-3.3.md for the
1503	  -- detailed chain (TBD). Each substep is project-side buildable on top of
1504	  -- Mathlib's Weil-divisor apparatus and the group-object API; substep 4
1505	  -- (pole-divisor intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1) is the
1506	  -- substantive Mathlib gap.
1507	  --
1508	  -- * Substep 1: construct the difference rational map
1509	  --   `Φ := (id × inv) ∘ (f × f) ∘ m : X × X ⇢ G`, dense on `U × U` for
1510	  --   any representative `(U, φ_U) ∈ f`. Needs the binary product on
1511	  --   `GrpObj`-objects and the right-monoidal-cat structure used in this
1512	  --   project (`MonoidalCategory.tensorObj`, `MonObj.mul`).
1513	  -- * Substep 2: `(x, x) ∈ Dom(Φ) ↔ x ∈ Dom(f)` via the formula
1514	  --   `f(x) = Φ(x, u) · f(u)` for any `u ∈ Dom(f)` in a chosen open
1515	  --   neighbourhood — direct from the group-law continuity + openness of
1516	  --   the domain of definition.
1517	  -- * Substep 3: pullback of the local ring at the identity `e ∈ G`:
1518	  --   `Φ` defined at `(x, x)` ⟺ `Φ^* (𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X, (x,x)}`. Uses the
1519	  --   `Scheme.RationalMap`-to-function-field machinery (the same gap that
1520	  --   blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` at L744 below).
1521	  -- * Substep 4: on the nonsingular variety `X × X`, the pole divisor
1522	  --   `div(g)_∞` of any non-zero rational function `g ∈ Φ^*(𝒪_{G,e})` is
1523	  --   pure codim-1; its intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1 in
1524	  --   the diagonal (Hartshorne AG 9.2, Krull's principal-ideal theorem).
1525	  --   This is the substantive Mathlib gap: Mathlib's
1526	  --   `RamificationTheory.PrincipalIdeal` provides Krull's HPP for ideals
1527	  --   but the scheme-level codim-1 intersection-with-diagonal lemma is
1528	  --   not packaged.
1529	  --
1530	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: gated on (a) the function-field-pullback bridge for
1531	  -- `Scheme.RationalMap` (also blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`),
1532	  -- (b) the codim-1 pole-divisor / diagonal intersection lemma (Hartshorne
1533	  -- AG 9.2 scheme-level form).
1534	  sorry

1536	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
1537	
1538	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
1539	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
1540	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
1541	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
1542	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
1543	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
1544	witnesses are constructed in this project).
1545	
1546	### Status note (iter-179 Lane D, Path D2)
1547	
1548	The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
1549	pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement
1550	
1551	> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
1552	> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
1553	> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,
1554	
1555	which requires the pullback machinery
1556	`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
1557	`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
1558	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the
1559	`RationalMap`-to-function-field ring map is **not** shipped by Mathlib
1560	at commit `b80f227`, and building it inside this file would constitute
1561	a substantive new sub-build (cf. `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
1562	on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).
1563	
1564	iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
1565	in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
1566	documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
1567	to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
1568	`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
1569	statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
1570	the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
1571	a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
1572	the pullback machinery lands.
1573	
1574	Blueprint reference (in its current weakened form): a definitional unfold
1575	of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`. -/
1576	
1577	/-- **Definedness at the generic point of a prime divisor, repackaged.**
1578	
1579	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `Y` a variety over `k̄`,
1580	`f : X ⇢ Y` a rational map, and `W : X.left.PrimeDivisor`, the following
1581	are equivalent:
1582	
1583	1. `W.point ∈ f.domain` (i.e.\ `f` is defined at the generic point of `W`).
1584	2. There exists a `PartialMap` representative `g` of `f` (a regular morphism
1585	   `g.hom : g.domain ⟶ Y.left` from a dense open `g.domain ⊆ X.left` with
1586	   `g.toRationalMap = f`) whose domain contains `W.point`.
1587	
1588	This is the canonical existential characterisation of `f.domain` membership
1589	via `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`, with the two
1590	conjuncts under the existential reordered so the `toRationalMap = f`
1591	component lands first (matching the way later witnesses in this project
1592	construct partial-map representatives).
1593	
1594	**Iter-179 Lane D scope.** This statement is the auditor-178B Path D2 honest
1595	fallback: the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 "regular = no pole" Weil-divisor
1596	reformulation (Stacks `02ME`), which would tighten the right-hand side to
1597	an `ord_W ≥ 0` condition on every pulled-back regular function, is deferred
1598	to a future lemma that builds the missing `RationalMap → function-field`
1599	pullback machinery. See the section-level docstring above. -/
1600	theorem mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point
1601	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1602	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1603	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1604	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1605	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1606	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1607	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1608	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
1609	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (W : X.left.PrimeDivisor) :
1610	    W.point ∈ f.domain ↔
1611	      ∃ (g : X.left.PartialMap Y.left),
1612	        g.toRationalMap = f ∧ W.point ∈ (g.domain : Set X.left) := by
1613	  rw [Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain]
1614	  exact ⟨fun ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩ => ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩, fun ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩ => ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩⟩

9	/-!
10	# The `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` substrate (A.1.c.SubT)
11	
12	This file is the **A.1.c.SubT** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It records the dedicated
14	substrate on which the abelian-group instance of the relative Picard quotient
15	`Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) := Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` rests (the residual `sorry`
16	of `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`).
17	
18	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
19	infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
20	`[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three
21	ingredients on the Lean carrier:
22	
23	1. a binary tensor-product operation
24	   `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`;
25	2. the structure sheaf `O_X` as a designated unit object for `⊗`;
26	3. an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects, i.e.
27	   the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)` of an invertible sheaf.
28	
29	At Mathlib's pinned commit (`b80f227`), only a presheaf-level version of (1)
30	is available (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); (2) and (3) are present
31	as scheme-level objects, but the binary operation in (1) that ties them
32	together at the `Scheme.Modules` level is missing, and there is no
33	`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the
34	project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) + (3)
35	into a monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.
36	
37	## Status (iter-202 Lane TS — file-skeleton scaffold)

104	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
105	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
106	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
107	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).

112	the small Zariski site of `X`. -/

133	/-- **Monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
134

2142	/-! ## §7. Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay
2143	
2144	The consumer-facing input for A.4.a: every regular Noetherian local ring is
2145	Cohen–Macaulay (Stacks tag 00OD). The direct proof: pick a minimal
2146	generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of `𝔪` (where `d = dim R`), use that `R` is a
2147	domain (Stacks 00NQ) to start an `R`-regular sequence, and induct on
2148	dimension — each `R/(x_1, …, x_c)` is again regular of dimension `d - c`,
2149	so `x_1, …, x_d` is an `R`-regular sequence and `depth(R) ≥ d`. The reverse
2150	inequality `depth(R) ≤ dim R` is the standard depth bound (Stacks 00LK).
2151	
2152	Blueprint reference: `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` (Stacks tag 00OD). -/
2153	
2154	namespace CohenMacaulay

2156	/-! ### Helper 1 (axiom-clean): length-bound on regular sequences
2157	
2158	For a Noetherian local ring `R`, every `R`-regular sequence has length at most
2159	`ringKrullDim R`. This is the **upper bound** half of Stacks 00OD: it is the
2160	specialisation of the equality
2161	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) + rs.length = ringKrullDim R`
2162	(`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`) to the observation that
2163	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) ≥ 0` whenever the quotient is nontrivial, which it
2164	is precisely because `IsRegular` rules out `rs • ⊤ = ⊤`.
2165	
2166	iter-181 Lane G: closed kernel-clean. -/
2167	private lemma length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular
2168	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
2169	    {rs : List R} (h : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs) :
2170	    (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim R := by
2171	  have heq := ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular rs h
2172	  have hntq : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) := by
2173	    rw [Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff]
2174	    intro habs
2175	    apply h.top_ne_smul
2176	    change (⊤ : Submodule R R) = (Ideal.ofList rs) • ⊤
2177	    rw [habs]; simp
2178	  have hnn : (0 : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) :=
2179	    ringKrullDim_nonneg_of_nontrivial
2180	  calc (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞)
2181	      = 0 + (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) := by simp
2182	    _ ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) := by gcongr
2183	    _ = ringKrullDim R := heq

2185	/-! ### Helper 1.5 (iter-187 Lane G sub-lane G1): cotangent-image of `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`
2186	
2187	For a local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and `x ∈ 𝔪` with `x ∉ 𝔪²`, the image of `x` in the
2188	cotangent space `𝔪.Cotangent` is nonzero. This is the key positivity input
2189	for the iter-187 cotangent dim-drop lemma below.

2191	Axiom-clean: direct from `Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero`. -/

2193	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
2194	    (x : R) (hx : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
2195	    (hxnotsq : x ∉ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ^ 2) :
2196	    (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).toCotangent
2197	        (⟨x, hx⟩ : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Ideal R)) ≠ 0 := by
2198	  intro habs
2199	  exact hxnotsq
2200	    ((Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero (I := IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ⟨x, hx⟩).mp habs)

2202	/-! ### Helper 2.0 (iter-187 Lane G sub-lane G1): cotangent dim-drop on `R ⧸ (x)`
2203	
2204	**Statement.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`, the
2205	cotangent space of `R / (x)` has dimension one less than that of `R`:
2206	```
2207	finrank κ' (CotangentSpace (R/(x))) + 1 = finrank κ (CotangentSpace R)
2208	```
2209	where `κ = R / 𝔪` and `κ' = (R/(x)) / 𝔪'` are the two residue fields
2210	(canonically isomorphic via the natural quotient `R/𝔪 ≃ (R/(x))/𝔪'`).
2211	
2212	**Role.** This is the cotangent-space dim-drop building block for
2213	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` below: once Stacks 00NQ
2214	(regular local ⟹ domain) is filled in, this dim-drop is what upgrades
2215	`R/(x)` of dimension `k` back to `IsRegularLocalRing` via
2216	`IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
2217	
2218	**Proof structure** (Bourbaki-style via `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank`):
2219	1. The R-algebra map `π : R → R/(x)` induces a κ-linear surjection of
2220	   cotangent spaces `f : 𝔪.Cotangent → 𝔪'.Cotangent` via
2221	   `Ideal.mapCotangent_surjective_of_comap_eq` (which applies because
2222	   `(maximalIdeal R).comap π = ker π ⊔ maximalIdeal R = (x) ⊔ 𝔪 = 𝔪`).
2223	2. The kernel of `f` is the R-submodule generated by the image `x̄` of `x`
2224	   in `𝔪.Cotangent` (via `Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective` — the
2225	   kernel is `(Submodule.comap 𝔪.subtype ((x) ⊓ 𝔪)).map 𝔪.toCotangent`
2226	   which collapses to `Submodule.span R {x̄}` because `(x) ⊆ 𝔪`).
2227	3. The R-action on 𝔪.Cotangent factors through κ (it's a κ-vector space),
2228	   so this is also `Submodule.span κ {x̄}`.
2229	4. `x ∉ 𝔪²` ⟺ `x̄ ≠ 0` (Helper 1.5), giving
2230	   `finrank κ (Submodule.span κ {x̄}) = 1` via `finrank_span_singleton`.
2231	5. Combining via `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank` and the
2232	   `LinearMap.quotKerEquivOfSurjective` isomorphism, we get the formula.
2233	   The residue-field switch `κ → κ'` is handled by
2234	   `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`, which
2235	   reduces the κ-finrank to spanFinrank (a ring-theoretic invariant) and
2236	   the spanFinrank-equality is purely numeric.
2237	
2238	**iter-187 deliverable**: structural scaffold with the assembled body left
2239	as a single named typed sorry (the κ-subspace identification step). The
2240	preparatory lemma `toCotangent_ne_zero_of_not_mem_sq` lands axiom-clean.

2242	**iter-188+** body fill-in: instantiate steps (1)-(5) above. Key Mathlib API:
2243	* `Ideal.mapCotangent`, `Ideal.mapCotangent_surjective_of_comap_eq`,
2244	  `Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective` (Mathlib b80f227 ✓)
2245	* `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank` (Mathlib b80f227 ✓)
2246	* `finrank_span_singleton` (Mathlib b80f227 ✓)
2247	* `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`
2248	  (Mathlib b80f227 ✓) — bridges κ-finrank and κ'-finrank via spanFinrank.

2496	/-! ### Helper 2 (substantive typed `sorry`): system-of-parameters as a regular sequence
2497	
2498	For a regular local ring `R` of Krull dimension `d = (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank`,
2499	a minimal generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of the maximal ideal `𝔪` is an `R`-regular
2500	sequence. This is the **lower bound** half of Stacks 00OD.
2501	
2502	The proof in Stacks 00OD uses:
2503	1. A regular local ring is an integral domain (Stacks 00NQ).
2504	2. For each `c < d`, the quotient `R / (x_1, …, x_c)` is again a regular local
2505	   ring, of Krull dimension `d - c` (Krull's principal ideal theorem +
2506	   `lemma-one-equation`).
2507	
2508	Step (1) — `IsRegularLocalRing R ⟹ IsDomain R` — is **not present in Mathlib at
2509	the pinned commit** (`b80f227`); a `lean_leansearch` for "regular local ring is
2510	a domain" returns nothing relevant. Step (2) requires the regular-quotient
2511	inductive structure (Stacks 00NQ + height-one quotient regularity), which is
2512	similarly absent. Both are substantive multi-iter content.
2513	
2514	The signature here is the non-tautological existence statement: a list `rs : List R`
2515	with `rs.length = (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank`, `rs ⊆ maximalIdeal R`, and
2516	`IsRegular R rs`. Downstream, `of_regular` consumes this directly to close the
2517	`depth ≥ d` lower bound.
2518	
2519	iter-185 Lane G **STRUCTURAL SCAFFOLD**: the main lemma is structurally
2520	complete by strong induction on `n = spanFinrank R`. The inductive step is
2521	factored into `regularLocal_inductive_step` (axiom-clean: lift-and-cons
2522	assembly via Mathlib's `IsRegular.cons'`) plus the Mathlib-gap helper
2523	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` (typed `sorry`,
2524	Stacks 00NQ + 00NU consolidated). The substrate work (`IsRegularLocalRing ⟹
2525	IsDomain` + Krull's PIT for quotient-by-minimal-generator) is genuinely
2526	multi-iter; the iter-185 progress is the structural extraction of the
2527	Mathlib gap into a narrow, well-typed helper, leaving the assembly
2528	axiom-clean for the gap-fill iteration to finish in `~5 LOC` once the
2529	helper closes. -/

2531	/-! ### Narrow substrate gap (Stacks 00NQ): regular local ring is a domain
2532	
2533	The Auslander–Buchsbaum-route closure of "regular local ring is Cohen–Macaulay"
2534	ultimately reduces to **Stacks 00NQ**: every regular local Noetherian ring
2535	is an integral domain. Mathlib at the pinned commit `b80f227` does not expose
2536	this implication (`lean_leansearch` for "regular local ring is a domain"
2537	returns nothing relevant; `lean_loogle` for `IsRegularLocalRing _ → IsDomain _`
2538	returns empty).
2539	
2540	Stacks 00NQ proof sketch (induction on `dim R = spanFinrank 𝔪`):
2541	* Base `dim R = 0`: then `𝔪 = ⊥`, hence `R` is a field, hence a domain.
2542	* Step `dim R = d + 1 ≥ 1`:
2543	  - Pick `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²` (axiom-clean via Nakayama; see
2544	    `exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos` below).
2545	  - By the cotangent dim-drop (`finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ`,
2546	    iter-188 Lane G1 closure) **plus** Krull's principal ideal theorem
2547	    (`ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim_of_mem_jacobson`
2548	    gives the `≥` half), `R / (x)` is regular local of dim `d`.
2549	  - By induction, `R / (x)` is a domain, hence `(x)` is a prime ideal of `R`.
2550	  - Let `𝔭` be a minimal prime of `R` with `𝔭 ⊆ (x)`. For `y ∈ 𝔭`, write
2551	    `y = xz`; since `𝔭` is prime and `x ∉ 𝔭` (else `x ∈ 𝔭 ⊆ (x)` doesn't
2552	    immediately give a contradiction but combined with `dim R/(x) = d` and
2553	    `x ∈ 𝔭` height-zero we get `𝔭 = 0` which is the conclusion), we get
2554	    `z ∈ 𝔭`. Hence `𝔭 ⊆ x 𝔭`, so iterating, `𝔭 ⊆ ∩ x^n 𝔭 ⊆ ∩ 𝔪^n = 0`
2555	    (Krull intersection). So `𝔭 = 0`, and `R` is a domain.
2556	
2557	Full formalization is ~300 LOC (involves Krull's PIT, prime avoidance,
2558	Krull intersection, minimal-primes-are-finite); deferred to a future iter
2559	or Mathlib upstream contribution. -/

2561	/-- **Axiom-clean Nakayama witness.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`
2562	with `spanFinrank 𝔪 ≥ 1`, there exists `x ∈ 𝔪` with `x ∉ 𝔪²`.
2563	
2564	This is the "cotangent space is nonzero" content: by Nakayama, if `𝔪 ⊆ 𝔪²`
2565	then `𝔪 = 0` (so `spanFinrank 𝔪 = 0`), contradicting the hypothesis. -/
2566	private lemma exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos
2567	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
2568	    (h : 0 < (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank) :
2569	    ∃ x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R, x ∉ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2 := by
2570	  -- By contradiction: assume 𝔪 ⊆ 𝔪², then by Nakayama 𝔪 = 0, but spanFinrank 𝔪 ≥ 1.
2571	  by_contra h_neg
2572	  push Not at h_neg
2573	  have h𝔪_le_sq : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ≤ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2 := h_neg
2574	  -- 𝔪² = 𝔪 • 𝔪.
2575	  have hsq : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) ^ 2
2576	      = (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) := by
2577	    rw [sq, ← Ideal.smul_eq_mul]
2578	  have h𝔪_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R).FG :=
2579	    Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
2580	  -- Use Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot with N = ⊥.
2581	  have hjac : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ≤ (⊥ : Ideal R).jacobson :=
2582	    IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _
2583	  have h_le_smul : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R)
2584	      ≤ ⊥ ⊔ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) •
2585	        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) := by
2586	    rw [bot_sup_eq, ← hsq]; exact h𝔪_le_sq
2587	  have h𝔪_bot : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) ≤ ⊥ :=
2588	    Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot h𝔪_fg hjac h_le_smul
2589	  have h𝔪_eq_bot : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) = ⊥ := le_bot_iff.mp h𝔪_bot
2590	  have h_span : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0 := by
2591	    rw [h𝔪_eq_bot]; exact Submodule.spanFinrank_bot
2592	  omega
2593	
2594	/-! ### Helper for Stacks 00NQ base case (iter-190 Lane G, axiom-clean).
2595	
2596	For a Noetherian local ring `R` with `(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0`, the
2597	ring `R` is a field, hence a domain. The maximal ideal collapses to `⊥` via
2598	`Submodule.spanFinrank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot` (under FG), and
2599	`IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq` upgrades the resulting field-by-
2600	trivial-maximal-ideal characterisation to `IsField R`, from which
2601	`IsField.isDomain` gives `IsDomain R`. -/
2602	private lemma isDomain_of_isLocalRing_of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_zero
2603	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
2604	    (h : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0) : IsDomain R := by
2605	  have h𝔪_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).FG :=
2606	    Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
2607	  have h𝔪_bot : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R = ⊥ :=
2608	    (Submodule.spanFinrank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot h𝔪_fg).mp h
2609	  have hField : IsField R :=
2610	    IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq.mpr h𝔪_bot
2611	  exact hField.isDomain

2614	
2615	For a regular local Noetherian ring `R` of `spanFinrank 𝔪 = k + 1` and
2616	`x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`, the quotient `R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}` is again a regular local
2617	ring of `spanFinrank 𝔪' = k`.

2619	This is the **axiom-clean** counterpart of
2620	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ`: it avoids the

2622	by routing the dimension lower bound through

2624	that does NOT require `x` to be a non-zero-divisor — instead of
2625	`ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim`. -/
2626	lemma regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq
2627	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
2628	    [IsRegularLocalRing R] {k : ℕ}
2629	    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1)
2630	    (x : R) (hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
2631	    (hxNotSq : x ∉ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2) :
2632	    ∃ _ : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)),
2633	    ∃ _ : IsLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)),
2634	    ∃ _ : IsRegularLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)),
2635	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))).spanFinrank = k := by
2636	  -- Step 1: instances on R/(x).
2637	  have hxNonunit : ¬ IsUnit x := fun hu =>
2638	    (IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal.mpr hu) hxMem
2639	  have hspan_ne_top : (Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≠ ⊤ :=
2640	    Ideal.span_singleton_ne_top hxNonunit
2641	  haveI hNT : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
2642	    Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr hspan_ne_top
2643	  haveI hLR : IsLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
2644	    IsLocalRing.of_surjective' (Ideal.Quotient.mk _) Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
2645	  -- Step 2: cotangent dim drop → spanFinrank drop on R/(x).
2646	  have hcot := finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ x hxMem hxNotSq
2647	  have hR_cot_eq :
2648	      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
2649	          (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace R) = k + 1 := by
2650	    rw [← IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace R]
2651	    exact hdim
2652	  have hR'_cot_eq :
2653	      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)))
2654	          (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))) = k := by
2655	    have h := hcot
2656	    rw [hR_cot_eq] at h
2657	    omega

2659	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))).spanFinrank = k := by

## Must-fix-this-iter

None.

The `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` proof is closed with no accidental `sorry`/`admit`, no circular dependency, and no weakened-wrong definition. All 4 new helper lemmas and 4 §3.B bridge declarations are axiom-clean. The TensorObjSubstrate.lean stubs are well-typed with no shadow/conflict against existing Mathlib declarations. No excuse-comments in the sense of admitting wrong code were found.

---

## Major

- `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:40–42` — File header states "remaining five typed `sorry` bodies (`depth`, `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`, `depth_of_short_exact`, `auslander_buchsbaum_formula`, `CohenMacaulay.of_regular`) are substantive multi-iter content." A sorry-grep of the entire file in proof positions returns ZERO hits — ALL five are now closed. This header is severely outdated and will mislead the plan agent into thinking major content is still sorry.

- `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:144` — Docstring for `depth` says "the body stays a typed `sorry`." The actual body (L148–152) is a proper definition. Stale.

- `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:285,365` — Section docstring says "Residual `sorry`s (2 named inline branches)" and a `--` comment says "body remaining as `sorry`." The proof of `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` (L367–619) has both branches fully closed. Stale.

- `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:638` — "Body is kernel-clean modulo the typed sorry of `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`." Since that theorem is now closed, the qualification is stale.

- `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:1706–1713` — The `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` structural section header describes this as "a single named typed-`sorry` declaration." It is not. The proof is closed. Stale.

- `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:2043–2048` — Main theorem comment describes `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` as packaging the gap "into a single typed `sorry`." It doesn't. Stale.

- `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:2239` — `finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ` docstring: "assembled body left as a single named typed sorry." The proof body (L2276–2494) is closed. Stale.

- `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:2523` — "`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` (typed `sorry`, Stacks 00NQ + 00NU consolidated)." The grep confirms this declaration has no sorry body. Stale.

- `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:3387,3390` — Comments describe `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` as "the lower bound, typed `sorry`" and claim the only residual sorry in `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` is the named helper. The grep shows no sorry in proof positions. Stale.

- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean:146` — `noncomputable instance monoidalCategory … := sorry` is a typeclass instance with a sorry body. While all downstream callers are also sorry stubs, any future axiom-clean proof that unintentionally synthesizes `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` (e.g., via `inferInstance` in a new file) would silently succeed by using this sorry-instance, producing a false closed proof. This is a structural contamination risk.

---

## Minor

- `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:365` — The inline `-- body remaining as \`sorry\`.` comment is placed inside a proof tactic block, not a docstring. A reader parsing proof structure could mistake it for an annotation about the proof step, not a historical note. Recommend removal.

- `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:1707` — "rather than an opaque inline `sorry` at the case-split site" is referring to the old design. Now the proof IS at the case-split site and is not opaque. Confusing phrasing.

- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean:191–193` — `tensorObjOnProduct` has a non-sorry body that calls `tensorObj` (sorry) and `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` (sorry). The result is transitively sorry, which a reader might not immediately recognize given the non-sorry body. A short comment noting this would aid comprehension.

- `WeilDivisor.lean:584–586` — `have hcongr : ∀ {a b : X} (e : Inseparable a b), (X.presheaf.stalkCongr e).hom = X.presheaf.stalkSpecializes e.ge := by intros; rfl` — the `rfl` claim that `stalkCongr e = stalkSpecializes e.ge` is a definitional equality assertion. This is plausible but could be fragile if Mathlib changes the definition of `stalkCongr`. No immediate concern, minor code smell.

- `CodimOneExtension.lean` — The §3.B docstring (L959–984) is lengthy and accurate, but the four declarations at L994–1040 are arguably over-documented given their one-line proof bodies. No functional issue.

None. Every `sorry`-referencing comment found in the codebase falls into one of:

2. An accurate description of a pre-existing sorry stub at a designed residual site.

No comment in the form "this is wrong but we're leaving it" was found.

- **must-fix-this-iter**: 0

The principal concern is that `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` has accumulated ~10 major stale comments describing proof bodies as sorry when they are not, which will mislead the plan agent; these should be swept before the next planning iteration.
